title: Head of Compliance, Singapore
company_name: AIG Asia Pacific Insurance Pte. Ltd.
experience: 8 - 10+ years of relevant compliance experience in Compliance, Legal or Audit
educational_qualifications: A professional educational background; preferably legal or audit qualifications
required_skill_set: Strong technical knowledge of MAS regulations and reporting requirements
Understanding of insurance processes, including the applicability of key laws and regulations
Ability to work effectively within a matrix organization and with employees of all levels
Approachable, self-initiator, problem solving with the ability to confront issues diplomatically
Ability to work independently while assuming ownership of tasks

additional_sections.content: At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us in Head of Compliance, Singapore to take your Compliance career to the next level with a global market leader. Make your mark in Global Legal, Compliance and Regulatory (GLCR). Our team provides first class advice to colleagues and functions across the organization, ensuring AIG navigates an ever-changing landscape that spans state, federal, and international lines. The team impacts legal exposure, develops new products, and manages compliance frameworks. Reporting directly to the Chief Compliance Officer for APAC and China, you will be the senior compliance leader for AIG Singapore, responsible for the entire compliance framework, liaising with stakeholders and regulatory bodies including MAS. You will lead governance initiatives, develop policies, oversee sanctions screening, manage compliance records, conduct risk assessments, and prepare management reports. You will also work with data analytics teams, implement compliance frameworks, and support regional projects.

8 - 10+ years of relevant compliance experience in Compliance, Legal or Audit, preferably in an insurance company. Educational background should include legal or audit qualifications. Must have strong technical knowledge of MAS regulations, insurance processes, and the ability to work within a matrix organization. Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills are required. Ability to work independently and own tasks is essential.

AIG values in-person collaboration, offers comprehensive benefits through the Total Rewards Program, and promotes a culture of inclusion and diversity. The company is committed to equal opportunity employment and provides reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities.

additional_sections.content: Technical Assistant, Specialty At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as an Underwriter, Property to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. Make your mark in Talbot With offices in the U.S., London and Asia Pacific, Talbot is the managing agent of Syndicate 1183 at Lloyd’s. Syndicate 1183 underwrites a number of classes of business including war and terrorism, energy, marine, financial lines, cyber, transport, political risk, crisis management, kidnap & ransom, contingency, property and treaty reinsurance. Through its expert team of underwriters and a balanced, geographically-diverse portfolio of business, Talbot has earned a reputation as an industry-leading insurance and reinsurance specialist. How you will create an impact To ensure all documentation to be scanned and indexed into the DMS system that comes through the Core Team. To ensure data entry is in accordance with agreed guidelines, e.g. endorsements, quotes, promise lines To ensure consistent field use within the Core team. To ensure accuracy and the resolution of any grey areas, of data entry manuals or and escalating any Issues. To ensure accuracy and timeliness of entry for endorsements. Attend and prepare necessary documentation for Core team meetings and for the monthly Operations meeting. To be a SME and/or Super user on Underwriting Systems as required. Ensure both personal and allocated class(es) compliance with document retention policy. To build and maintain close working relationships with Underwriters and Capita. To act in accordance with all regulatory standards and principals Ad-hoc and general administration duties as and when required To run, update and upload the PMD report to Lloyds before the 15th of each month. Premium Management To prepare, produce and distribute weekly premium management reports such as large movement and signed variance reports. To liaise and work with all Underwriting Teams in all areas of Premium Management data integrity/ allocation and answer any queries they may have. Responsible for USM processing and corrections where applicable To process and validate risks signed through Xchanging; validation of all Underwriting Signing Messages (USMs). To carry out daily posting, to ensure end of month process posting deadlines are reached. Liaise with UTS, Underwriting, Xchanging and Brokers on processing queries, action and monitor until resolved. Identify and liaise with underwriters and chase as appropriate, for partial risks where premium has been processed through Xchanging. What you’ll need to succeed Excellent general computer literacy. Competent in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Outlook Detail-oriented Diploma / Degree in any field. Chartered Insurance Institute preferred. Able to operate independently with little direction or guidance. Self-reliance, use of initiative- a ‘self-starter’. Able to prioritize Strong team working approach. Strong analytical skills. Calm, rational and methodical approach Verbal, written and professional presence and ability to communicate at all levels with excellent negotiation skills Ready to take your career to the next level? We would love to hear from you. #LI-LY1 At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become.

title: Program Manager
company_name: Risk Specialists Companies Insurance Agency, Inc.
experience: 10+ Years of Commercial Underwriting Experience focused on Multi Line Package portfolio management.
required_skill_set: ISO, Admitted and Non Admitted product execution

additional_sections.content: At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Program Manager to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. Make your mark in Programs. With over 50 years in the Programs space, AIG Programs is an industry leader in the U.S. Program business. We focus on developing strategic solutions designed to make Program Administrators’ lives easier. By leveraging our breadth of offerings and dedicated Underwriting, Claims and Loss Control teams, we offer exceptional service and solutions. We serve niche markets on both an admitted and non-admitted basis, through market leading infrastructure and underwriting expertise. How you will create an impact: A Program Manager is highly motivated, results oriented problem solver who exemplifies a willingness to be flexible, learn on the job and maintain a “can do” attitude. Program Manager is responsible for the underwriting, marketing & portfolio management of each assigned Program. Developing and negotiating business plans for each assigned program, including plans for new business, renewal rate management, expected retentions to position the division to achieve profit objectives. Participating in Underwriting Quality Reviews; conduct additional file reviews as needed. Positioning program growth in jurisdictions that are deemed rate adequate and managing to the technically correct price. Coordinating and ‘chair’ Stewardship meetings for assigned Programs. Identifying and marketing new products to round out existing portfolios and incrementally enhance portfolio of products afforded to our clients. Monitoring renewals, rate targets vs. achieved, retentions, trends, lost business, etc. on a monthly basis to assure achievement of business plan and profitability targets. Understanding each assigned program: the industries and their exposures to loss, updating program ‘eligibility’ and coverages afforded as needed to minimize adverse exposures to unanticipated loss. Actively identify training opportunities at the PA level to enhance their LOB knowledge and successfully guide Program Administrators in their execution on risk selection, pricing, coverage terms and conditions in a manner that is aligned with authorities granted by AIG. Actively understanding and managing the authority delegated by the PA to the underwriters assigned to our program(s). Oversight and monitor PA ‘peers review’ process. Developing a strong working knowledge of AIG operating systems (rating and policy issuance) at the PA level. Understanding, managing and minimizing AAL for each program. Developing line of business expertise for assigned Program(s). Identifying trends in the program profiles, communicating & implementing appropriate measures by LOB in order to protect the portfolio and AIG from adverse exposures. Demonstrating an understanding of industry changes and potential impact by r LOB through clearly articulated recommendations for actions during the account referral process. Ensuring Program Administrator is meeting AIG’s underwriting expectations and is aligned with program business plans through the execution and management of their underwriting authority including the participation in file reviews, UQR Audits and Stewardship meetings. Participating in insurance & industry specific association training/meetings to enhance knowledge by class of business to stay current with industry specific market trends as it pertains to assigned Program(s). Collaborating with Actuarial, Accounting, Claims, Operations, Program Development, Risk Management and Underwriting as necessary. Overseeing the implementation of new business programs assigned to you including coordination with Operations, IT and other functional support areas. Maintaining current industry knowledge, market intelligence and competitor analysis through regular communication with assigned Program Administrator(s) and participation in external conferences, trade shows, etc.

title: Underwriter Casualty (w/m/d)
experience: Not specified
educational_qualifications: Abgeschlossene akademische Ausbildung vorzugsweise mit juristischem Schwerpunkt oder mit Versicherungsfachwissen
required_skill_set: Berufserfahrung im Bereich Industrieversicherung oder Haftpflicht bei einem Versicherer oder Versicherungsmakler mit Fokus auf Industrierisiken
Berufserfahrung in einer Rechtsanwaltskanzlei mit Tätigkeitsfeld Zivil- und/oder Versicherungsrecht
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
salary: EUR 56,454.58 per year (minimum, with potential for overpayment depending on qualification)

additional_sections.content: Seit über 100 Jahren stehen wir weltweit für unsere Expertise zur Absicherung komplexer Risiken. In diesem partnerschaftlichen Sinne unterstützen wir unsere Kunden dabei, einer ungewissen Zukunft vertrauensvoll entgegenzublicken. In Österreich ist die AIG seit 70 Jahren aktiv und steht den Versicherten seitdem mit einem lokalen Team versierter Experten in Wien jederzeit zur Seite. Bei uns werden Sie nicht nur Teil eines ambitionierten Teams bei einem der weltweit führenden Versicherungsunternehmen, sondern haben gleichzeitig die Möglichkeit, an der Gestaltung nationaler und internationaler Risikotransfers hautnah beteiligt zu sein. Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams in Wien suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen neuen Kollegen (m/w/d) für die Position des Underwriter Casualty (w/m/d) mit einem Pensum von 80 % bis 100 % zur Verstärkung unseres Teams am Standort Wien.

Underwriting: Prüfung und Bewertung von Haftpflichtrisiken für Industrieunternehmen und globale österreichische Akteure. Laufende Betreuung des bestehenden Portfolios und Angebotslegung für potenzielle Neukunden. Analyse und Dokumentation hinsichtlich Deckungsqualität, Profitabilität, interner Guidelines und Regeln sowie individuellen Risikoprofilen der Unternehmen. Verhandlung der Bedingungen und Konditionen mit Maklern und Kunden.
Vertrieb: Aufbau und Pflege einer vertrauensvollen und engen Partnerschaft mit den relevanten Maklerhäusern in Österreich. Strategische Akquisition gemeinsamen mit dem Client und Broker Management für Österreich. Repräsentation der AIG an Events und Fachveranstaltungen.
Inhaltliche Arbeit: Laufende Weiterentwicklung der AIG-Bedingungen sowie Beobachtung von Gesetzgebung und Rechtsprechung hinsichtlich Haftungs- und Versicherungsrechtlicher Themen. Aufbau eines tieferen Verständnisses für Industrien und den typischen Risiken und Haftungsszenarien.
Arbeit im Team: Enge kollegiale Zusammenarbeit in allen zuvor genannten Bereichen. Engmaschige Einarbeitung sowohl in die Kerndisziplin Underwriting als auch in die Systeme und Governance der AIG.
Blick über den Tellerrand: Neben den klassischen Betriebshaftpflichtrisiken besteht auch die Möglichkeit im Bereich Energy, Construction und CPI (Produktschutzversicherung) Erfahrungen zu sammeln und in weiterer Folge Verantwortung zu übernehmen.

Abgeschlossene akademische Ausbildung vorzugsweise mit juristischem Schwerpunkt oder mit Versicherungsfachwissen.
Berufserfahrung im Bereich Industrieversicherung oder Haftpflicht bei einem Versicherer oder Versicherungsmakler mit Fokus auf Industrierisiken oder Berufserfahrung in einer Rechtsanwaltskanzlei mit Tätigkeitsfeld Zivil- und/oder Versicherungsrecht.
Selbstständiges Arbeiten, hohe Selbstmotivation und gute Auffassungsgabe
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift.

Wir bieten ein marktkonformes Gehalt. Für die ausgeschriebene Stelle kommt ein kollektivvertragliches jährliches Bruttomindestentgelt von EUR 56,454.58 bei einer wöchentlichen Arbeitszeit von 38,5 Stunden (Vollzeittätigkeit) zur Anwendung, wobei jedoch in Abhängigkeit Ihrer konkreten Qualifikation Bereitschaft zur Überzahlung besteht. Wir bieten vielfältige Aufgaben in einem dynamischen und internationalen Umfeld. Zudem investieren wir in unsere Mitarbeitenden durch kontinuierliche Weiterbildung und eine Kultur der Inklusion.
AIG legt Wert auf Inklusion und Gleichberechtigung. Wir bieten angemessene Vorkehrungen für Bewerber mit Behinderungen. Für Anfragen bezüglich Unterstützung wenden Sie sich an candidatecare@aig.com.

title: Reserving & Analytics Actuary, APAC
experience: Extensive general insurance reserving experience, preferably in long tail lines
required_skill_set: Strong analytical and problem solving skills
Ability to work as part of a team
Working knowledge on actuarial reserving software

additional_sections.content: #LI-JC1 At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Reserving & Analytics Actuary to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. Make your mark in Actuarial Our Actuaries are on the front lines, quantifying risk, pricing insurance and reserving losses for AIG’s major insurance operating companies, globally. We collaborate with Finance, Claims, Operations, Marketing and Risk Management, among other functions. As an AIG actuary, you will be challenged and encouraged to reach your greatest potential to help improve how we predict, manage and mitigate risk. How you will create an impact As an actuary at AIG, you will be challenged and encouraged to reach your greatest potential. Every day will bring new opportunities to stretch your analytical and problem-solving skills as you improve how we predict and mitigate risk. Our Financial Actuaries are on the frontlines, quantifying risk, pricing insurance and reserving losses for AIG’s major regulated and rated insurance operating companies globally. Portfolio Analytics and Pricing Actuaries support AIG’s businesses and are responsible for technical pricing, product development analytics and performance measurement. They collaborate with Marketing, Operations and Financial Management. The successful candidate will be supporting the AIG APAC insurance business, and work as a member of the APAC actuarial team to achieve goals and deliver on strategic initiatives. The candidate is expected to work as part of the regional team to provide analytical insights to the business functions including profit centres, underwriting, finance, and risk management and also provide local regulatory support for the assigned APAC countries. Key responsibilities include: As a member of the APAC regional actuarial team, coordinate US GAAP quarterly management reporting requirements with the regional tower leads: Assist in supporting strategic portfolio reviews and deep dive analyses of key business segments within APAC; Preparation of local regulatory reporting for AIG’s Australia and New Zealand offices. This includes: Quarterly Board and Risk committee reporting; Quarterly solvency calculations and IFRS 17 disclosures Annual Insurance Liability Valuation Report and Financial Condition / Stress Testing Report; Review of capital projections and investment strategies including ALM (“Asset Liability Matching); Provide support to the budget loss ratio exercise and annual financial planning; Assist in other ad-hoc analyses as required. What you’ll need to succeed Extensive general insurance reserving experience, preferably in long tail lines. Proficiency in actuarial concepts/methodologies and ability to educate non-technical managers on technical areas that enhance their decision-making ability; Strong analytical and problem solving skills; Effective project & time management and strong communication skills; Ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously; Ability to work as part of a team as well as take responsibility for his individual contribution Ability to meet tight deadlines and prioritise own work; Ability to forge relationships with other areas of the business; Working knowledge on typical actuarial reserving software; Professional behaviour with high level of integrity; Applicants must have valid rights to work in Australia, as employer sponsorship is not available for this position. title: Senior Underwriter Trade Credit (w/m/d)
experience: Mehrjährige Erfahrung im Banken- und/oder Versicherungsbereich, bevorzugt in Kreditversicherung, Working Capital / Receivables Finance
educational_qualifications: Studium der Rechts-, Wirtschaftswissenschaften oder eines vergleichbaren Studiengangs

Fundierte PC-Kenntnisse, insbesondere in Word, Excel und PowerPoint

additional_sections.content: Seit über 100 Jahren stehen wir weltweit für unsere Expertise zur Absicherung komplexer Risiken. In diesem partnerschaftlichen Sinne unterstützen wir unsere Kunden dabei, einer ungewissen Zukunft vertrauensvoll entgegenzublicken. In Deutschland ist die AIG seit 70 Jahren aktiv und steht den Versicherten seitdem mit lokalen Teams versierter Experten an vier Standorten jederzeit zur Seite. Bei uns werden Sie nicht nur Teil eines ambitionierten Teams bei einem der weltweit führenden Versicherungsunternehmen, sondern haben gleichzeitig die Möglichkeit, an der Gestaltung nationaler und internationaler Risikotransfers hautnah beteiligt zu sein. Für die Position des Senior Underwriter (w/m/d) Trade Credit suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen neuen Kollegen (m/w/d) zur Verstärkung unseres Teams am Standort Frankfurt am Main. Was Sie erwartet: Underwriting von nationalen und internationalen Risiken im Bereich Trade Credit Multinational & Strategic Accounts Ausbau und Steuerung eines Trade Credit Portfolios mit Schwerpunkt auf die DACH-Region Akquise und Betreuung in Koordination mit dem Vertriebsteam und Vertriebspartnern Erarbeitung von Renewal-Strategien inklusive der Verhandlungen Prüfung von Wordings Erstellung von Reports zur Entwicklung des Portfolios Koordination von internationalen Programmen mit anderen Stakeholdern innerhalb der AIG.

Wir bieten Ihnen vielfältige Aufgaben in einem digitalen und internationalen Umfeld mit flexiblen Arbeitszeiten. Wir arbeiten in flachen Hierarchien mit echten Gestaltungsmöglichkeiten. Teamarbeit steht bei uns an oberster Stelle. Regelmäßige Diversity & Inclusion-Veranstaltungen sowie Corporate Benefits und Fahrradleasing runden unsere Arbeitswelt ab. Zusatzleistungen wie 30 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr, arbeitsfreie Tage am 24.12. und 31.12., betriebliche Altersvorsorge und vermögenswirksame Leistungen sind für uns selbstverständlich.

title: Senior Underwriter Construction
company_name: AIG Europe S.A.- General Representative for Italy
experience: Proven underwriting experience, with good knowledge of Construction

additional_sections.content: Senior Construction Underwriter Join us as a Senior Underwriter to make a greater impact through your technical expertise and people skills. This could be the ideal chance to step up into your Senior level role. Make your mark in Underwriting AIG underwriting teams help to find insurance solutions in areas including Financial Lines, Property, Casualty, Specialty Lines, Cyber, Multinational Clients, and High Net Worth individuals. We are reimagining how we help customers to manage risk, transforming our operating model, and reshaping our role responsibilities and career pathways. The goal is to unlock the full potential in each colleague – empowering our people to grow as insurance professionals and add more value to our customers and AIG. How you will create an impact This role is your opportunity to drive profitable growth in Construction, by working on creative solutions for complex risks. You will be responsible for underwriting a range of new and renewal business, using your proven relationship building skills. We want you to work closely with brokers to communicate risk appetite and our value proposition – and to understand the deal structures, terms and products that best meets clients’ needs. You will then negotiate pricing, terms & conditions, and deal structure with brokers and clients. It will be important to generate new business by expanding your broker network, attending broker events, and building new relationships across the market. You will collaborate with AIG Distribution, territory managers, and underwriters in other business lines to target desirable accounts and cross-selling opportunities. As a senior member of the team, you will set a high standard of customer service to brokers and clients – providing an example for junior colleagues to follow. You will also provide guidance, mentoring and training to less-experienced underwriters – and support them in building their broker network. What you’ll need to succeed Proven underwriting experience, with good knowledge of Construction. The ability to analyze financial statements. Strong communication, networking and relationship building skills. Good organizational and time management skills. Customer and Sales orientated behaviors: motivated, tenacious, focused, technically skilled, proactive, and accountable. An interest in mentoring and training if you have not held these duties previously. Ready to step up to new challenges? We would love to hear from you. At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. We created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become. Welcome to a culture of inclusion We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories. AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com.

title: Student Worker Financial Lines
company_name: AIG Europe S.A Filial i Sverige
experience: Entry level
educational_qualifications: Universitetsstudent inom juridik, ekonomi eller liknande
required_skill_set: Behärskar svenska och engelska i tal och skrift

additional_sections.content: Är du student och intresserad av ett flexibelt och meriterande extrajobb vid sidan av studierna? Vill du prova dina vingar och få arbetserfarenhet i en dynamisk och internationell miljö? Hos AIG har du möjlighet att utvecklas och att fördjupa ditt intresse inom finans, försäkring och juridik. Just nu söker vi två universitetsstudenter för att stödja det svenska Financial Lines teamet. AIG är globalt marknadsledande inom området Financial Lines, ett produktområde som arbetar med att försäkra organisationers olika finansiella risker. Teamet bedömer och prissätter risker och anpassar försäkringsprodukten enligt kundens behov. Det nordiska teamet är baserade i Stockholm, Köpenhamn och i Helsingfors och du kommer även att ha kontakt med experter baserade på våra kontor runt om i världen. Som Student Worker inom Financial Lines kommer du bidra till tillväxten av teamet genom att hjälpa våra Underwriters. Du kommer att få kontakt med både interna och externa stakeholders då vi strävar efter att ge bästa möjliga service till våra mäklare och kunder. Detta är ett flexibelt extrajobb för dig som studerar på universitetet. För dig som vill visa framfötterna och tar initiativ kan det finnas möjligheter även efter dina studier.

Vänligen skicka in din ansökan via vår karriärportal så snart som möjligt. Rekrytering sker löpande. Vi ser gärna att din ansökan är på engelska. Om du har frågor kring positionen är du välkommen att kontakta Helene Foxberg, Head of Management Liability Commercial, Nordics / +46 (0)73 556 6926.

American International Group, Inc. (AIG) är en global ledare inom kommersiella och personliga försäkringslösningar; vi är ett av världens mest omfattande nätverk för egendomsskador. Vi investerar i våra största tillgångar, våra människor, genom kontinuerlig lärande och utveckling, i en kultur som hyllar alla för den de är och vad de vill bli.

additional_sections.content: Reporting to the Assistant Director, Actuarial Controllership, the Supervisory Accountant, Financial Reporting serves a critical role within Actuarial Controllership in ensuring timely and accurate delivery of the quarterly financial close and reporting as it relates to General Insurance loss reserves along with the maintenance of a robust control environment. This role will serve as a key liaison between AIG’s Controllership team and the global Actuarial function, setting standards, delivering on key reporting requirements of both constituencies, and ensuring that critical close timelines are met. The Supervisory Accountant, Actuarial Financial Reporting will work closely with the Actuarial Controllership team in the United States, as well as sharing oversight of a wider team based in Bangalore, India. Key Responsibilities include ensuring proper accounting and reporting of P&C loss reserve estimates, review of reconciliations, supporting SOX control execution, producing external disclosures, ensuring audit package accuracy, process improvement, and supporting transformation initiatives.

Bachelors Degree in Accounting, 3+ years of experience in finance roles in the Property & Casualty industry, strong operational and technical U.S. GAAP accounting experience, problem-solving capabilities, experience in a dynamic environment, strong business and financial acumen, excellent communication skills, and a history of process improvement.

title: Senior Underwriter Marine
experience: Relevant and extensive experience in Marine insurance underwriting
educational_qualifications: Bachelors degree: Business Administration or Economics university degree preferred. Master in Marine Insurance will be a Plus.
required_skill_set: Fluent English and Spanish skills (written & spoken)

additional_sections.content: Senior Underwriter Marine Make your mark in Underwriting AIG underwriting teams help to find insurance solutions in areas including Financial Lines, Property, Casualty, Environmental, Marine, Specialty Lines, Cyber, Multinational Clients, and High Net Worth individuals. We are reimagining how we help customers to manage risk, transforming our operating model, and reshaping our role responsibilities and career pathways. The goal is to unlock the full potential in each colleague – empowering our people to grow as insurance professionals and add more value to our customers and AIG. About the role What you need to know: The Marine Sr. Underwriter will demonstrate significant and extensive technical analysis and a great underwriting acumen combined with marketing and sales skills to meet production underwriting goals. This position will be a primary contributor to the development and production of a profitable portfolio, a key member of a high performing team, and overall underwriting and execution for AIG. This position will maintain strong broker and client relationships and be responsible for a sub-set of the overall profitability of the business. Candidates are expected to work effectively in a team environment in addition to working independently to manage a profitable book of new and renewal business.

Underwriting and Operational Excellence: Execute the agreed strategy, business objectives and target profitability. Maintain consistent underwriting best practices and guidelines to ensure rating and pricing adequacy. Underwrite new and existing business in accordance with underwriting authority. Negotiate pricing, terms and conditions & deal structures. Technically evaluate risks to help achieve profitable growth to the portfolio. Review and negotiate wordings and coverages. Identify cross-selling opportunities with different Lines of Business. Maintain and monitor appropriate aggregate exposure by sub-classes, insureds, and geography. Ensure that the administration of the document management and workflow systems are carried out in accordance to internal procedures. Demonstrate a consistent understanding of regulatory and compliance processes. Act in accordance with all regulatory standards and principles at all times. Assist in identifying trends in the portfolio, communicating & implementing appropriate measures in order to protect the portfolio from adverse exposures. Management of a book of business including broker plans, target account lists, pipelines, and action plans for poor performing accounts. Participate in special underwriting projects as needed.

Participate in and lead broker engagement plans, ultimately driving submission flow that specifically fits our desired appetite. Execute business development and marketing strategies to generate new, and continuously develop existing, business relationships. Communicate risk appetite and value proposition to brokers. Provide superior customer service to brokers and clients. Help to develop products and services to producers. Collaborate with distribution partners to be visible on a weekly basis.

Promote collaboration, diversity and inclusion within the team. Ability to interact and find success in navigating various cultures and situational challenges. Participate in industry functions and events to further build the Talbot brand identity and marketplace presence.

Relevant and extensive experience in Marine insurance underwriting. Bachelors degree: Business Administration or Economics university degree preferred. Master in Marine Insurance will be a Plus. Fluent English and Spanish skills (written & spoken). Analytical and quantitative skills. Knowledge of Risks analysis methodologies. Outgoing and entrepreneurial personality. Good communication and interpersonal skills. Negotiating, communications and presentations skills. Organizational and timekeeping skills and a strong sense of attention to detail. Customer focus. Results driven.

title: Senior Data & Reporting Analyst
experience: Experience in a similar role within the insurance industry
required_skill_set: Strong coding skills in R and / or Python
Experience of enhancing and automating processes
Understanding of cat modelling and exposure management advantageous

additional_sections.content: Join us as a Senior Data & Reporting Analyst in our Exposure Management team to take your career to the next level with a global market leader. Talbot operates within the Lloyd's Insurance Market through Syndicates 1183, 2019, S2478 and 2479. With an expert team of highly skilled and experienced underwriters and a balanced, geographically diverse portfolio of business, Talbot has earned a reputation as an industry leading insurance specialist. Talbot writes a diversified portfolio including Crisis Management, Energy, Financial Lines, Marine, Political Risk, Property and Terrorism. Drawing on our deep industry expertise, Talbot develops tailored insurance solutions that address clients' evolving and often unique needs. How you will create an impact This is an exciting role that will do important and impactful work across Cat Modelling and Exposure Management. Some of the key responsibilities include: Review regulatory reporting, assess opportunities for automation and implement improvements to make sure that they can be produced quickly and consistently Review and develop the suite of reports available to underwriting teams to support aggregate management activities and monitoring of risk appetites Improve data capture across all classes through standardisation of data entry and data checking processes Ensure that an appropriate level of focus is maintained on improving data quality across different classes, and develop and enhance data capture for classes that do not have specific mechanisms in place Support the timely delivery of regulatory returns and take responsibility for compiling data for both internal and external reporting Provide information from the aggregate monitoring process to assist with reinsurance planning and ensure close collaboration with the underwriting and outwards reinsurance teams to improve data completeness Support the development of Talbot’s views of catastrophe risk and feed into development / validation activities (including linkage into AIG group work streams) Assist in developing and reviewing the representation of non-nat-cat classes in the Internal Model Help to develop oversight frameworks and own procedural documentation for the Exposure Management team Stay up-to-date with industry developments and investigate ways in which we can enhance our data capture.

additional_sections.content: Join us as a D&O/FI Complex Claims Adjuster to grow your experience in handling complex claims. Our Claims teams are the proven problem solvers of choice for clients, delivering consistent technical excellence and showcasing our service differentiation to create an unparalleled global claims handling experience. Through a robust stakeholder feedback loop and supported by consistent processes and leadership, we take pride in delivering responsive, fair and professional service with empathy and efficiency. How you will create an impact: Focused on Directors & Offices and Financial Institutions claims within Financial Lines, this role is an ideal opportunity to develop your expertise in handling and investigating complex claims. The aim is deal with genuine claims through a proactive, best practice approach, in line with AIGs quality standards – you will actively promote and demonstrate the principles of ‘Treating Customers Fairly’ in claims handling. You will also proactively identify and review suspect/fraudulent claims on a consistent basis – dealing with such claims in line with our clear guidelines. Your work will involve regular contact with internal stakeholders and brokers, experts, and external lawyers. We want you to build effective relationships with internal and external customers, while acting as a point of referral. You will have the opportunity to grow your experience by providing support to Team Managers and deputizing in their absence. For example, you will assist with monitoring market/industry practices in claims handling. In addition, you can use the claims experience you gain to help improve our products in collaboration with colleagues in underwriting.

American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. title: FNOL Cabin Agent
company_name: AIG SEGUROS MEXICO S.A. DE C.V.
experience: 1 year in customer support, basic knowledge of insurance and claims processes
required_skill_set: Follow-up of procedures in customer service

additional_sections.content: FNOL Cabin Agent Who we are. American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a leading global insurance organization. AIG member companies offer a wide range of casualty and casualty insurance in approximately 70 countries and jurisdictions. This diverse offering includes products and services that help businesses and individuals protect their assets and manage risk. We are also committed to making a positive difference for our colleagues and the communities where we work and live. We encourage our colleagues to contribute to the causes that matter most to them by supporting these initiatives through our Volunteer Time Off Programs and Matching Grants. Get to know the business General Insurance is a leading provider of insurance products and services for business and personal customers. It has one of the most extensive property insurance networks in the world. General Insurance offers a wide range of products to its customers through a diversified, multi-channel distribution network. The Customer Service team collaborates with the company to support policyholders and brokers on a wide range of products and offers, through each interaction during 24/7 business hours, 365 days a year. Each CC agent is responsible for delivering and materializing the product offer and promise. Our main objective is to provide support, experience and an excellent service in emergency care. The FNOL Cabin Agent has the responsibility to provide immediate attention to the requests of our policyholders, even in situations where health and safety are compromised. On paper The FNOL Cabin Agent is in charge of providing immediate and quality attention to our policyholders when they suffer a vehicle or home accident or require any type of assistance that we offer in our products. The position is part of the Mexico Contact Center team, responsible for assisting our customers during a claim or who need assistance with a 24/7 service schedule, in accordance with local regulations and the criteria established by AIG guidelines. In 2025, FNOL's Claims and Assistance Contact Center in Mexico answered approximately 200,000 calls. The main activities of this agent are: Register new claims in our systems due to traffic accidents, roadside assistance, etc. Carry out the correct validation of the status and collection of the policy. Customer service support, providing the necessary information to follow up on your claim. What you need to know: The contact center is not only essential to support the business team and attend to the insured in case of claim or assistance, but also to avoid financial and regulatory impacts. In 2025 it achieved KPIs above the market with 90% in Service Level and 3% in Abandoned calls. AUTO: Call option: 96,444 options / Portfolio value: USD 33 million in annualized premiums. Package: Call option: 10,320 options / Portfolio value: USD 5 million in annualized premiums. Collateral: Call option: 85,812 options / Portfolio value: USD 16 million in annualized premiums. Positional performance has a direct positive impact in recent years on insured NPS survey results from 53% in 2020 to 64% in 2025. Your contribution to AIG The FNOL Cabin Agent is tasked with gathering detailed information to provide the care of car accidents, personal accidents, business lines and multiple assistance. Through knowledge of the product and care processes, as well as the correct use of our main systems, to meet the needs of our policyholders at the moment of truth and avoid an economic or reputational impact.

Work closely with an outsourced team to timely production of the regulatory returns Provide regular feedback and where necessary train the outsourced team. To conduct analytical reviews on judgements and assumptions used for the regulatory reporting including developing and documenting policy methodologies to support the regulatory reporting.

additional_sections.content: 担当先企業が保有する個人顧客向けの自動車・個人火災・各種の人保険といった伝統的な保険商品の推進に加え、今後拡大が期待される延長保証保険・約定履行保険等を活用した創造的なソリューション提供を通じて、企業営業本部の成長基盤と収益を拡大していくことが部門の目的です。大手金融機関や製造・運輸・建設・住宅関連の事業会社等の大手企業顧客と、その傘下の保険代理店（企業代理店）が主な担当先です。お客様に誠実に向き合いながら、企業代理店と信頼関係を築き、共に成長できる環境を作っていくことが、営業担当者に期待される重要な役割です。
企業代理店の皆様に、AIGの有する様々なパーソナル系損害保険商品のValueをご理解頂き、それを活かして、お客様に最適なソリューションを提案する。社内外の関係者との良好なコミュニケーションを通じて、チームとして収益を伴う成長に向けた新しい企画や施策を検討し、実行する。所属するチーム内での情報共有、各種施策の実行計画の立案、人材育成。
内部関係先 (AIG組織内): 商品（引受）部門及び損害サービス部門、企業営業本部傘下各部門。外部関係先 (AIG組織外): 企業代理店、その他の保険代理店、保険仲立人、顧客企業。
最終学歴：学士号または同等の学位。スキル：PCスキル（Excel/Word/PPT中級以上尚可）、ソーシャルスキル、社内外との対人コミュニケーションスキル、保険知識（あれば尚可）。業務経験：法人営業経験があれば尚可。生保で大企業・中堅企業へ団体保険の販売経験のある方や、企業代理店で業務災害保険・延長保証保険・約定履行保険等の販売経験や、職域営業の経験のある方も尚可。

title: Associate Underwriter M&A (w/m/d)
experience: First relevant experience in M&A and insurance/banking
educational_qualifications: Master's degree in law, engineering, or economics; MBA, LL.M. preferred

additional_sections.content: Associate Underwriter M&A (w/m/d) Standort: Frankfurt a.M. / München Seit über 100 Jahren stehen wir weltweit für unsere Expertise zur Absicherung komplexer Risiken. In diesem partnerschaftlichen Sinne unterstützen wir unsere Kunden dabei, einer ungewissen Zukunft vertrauensvoll entgegenzublicken. In Deutschland ist die AIG seit 70 Jahren aktiv und steht den Versicherten seitdem mit lokalen Teams versierter Experten an vier Standorten jederzeit zur Seite. Bei uns werden Sie nicht nur Teil eines ambitionierten Teams bei einem der weltweit führenden Versicherungsunternehmen, sondern haben gleichzeitig die Möglichkeit, an der Gestaltung nationaler und internationaler Risikotransfers hautnah beteiligt zu sein. Für die Position des Associate Underwriter M&A (w/m/d) suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen neuen Kollegen (m/w/d) zur Verstärkung unseres Teams am Standort Frankfurt oder München. Was Sie erwartet: Koordination des Underwriting-Prozesses von der Anfrage bis zum Abschluss der Police Unterstützung bei der Risikobewertung von M&A-Transaktionen im Hinblick auf die Gewährleistungen des Verkäufers Vorbereitung von Standardverträgen (Indikationen, Vertraulichkeits- und Beitrittsvereinbarungen, Expense Agreements, Policen) Teilnahme an und Nachbearbeitung von Underwriting Calls Erstellen und Unterstützung des Verhandelns der Police mit Kunden und deren Anwälten Eigenverantwortliche Übernahme von begleitenden administrativen und organisatorischen Aufgaben in Zusammenhang mit dem Underwriting-Prozess unter der Erstellung und Buchung von Policen Unterstützung bei der Koordination und Korrespondenz mit Maklern und externen Rechtsberatern sowie der Abstimmung mit ausländischen Kollegen Pflege von Beziehungen zu Private Equity Unternehmen, Inhouse M&A Abteilungen, Investment Banking Teams, spezialisierten Versicherungsmaklern und Anwaltskanzleien Unterstützung des Head of M&A Northern Europe und der Schadensabteilung bei operativen Aufgaben wie z.B. Erstellung und fortlaufende Auswertung von Statistiken Unterstützung des Head of M&A Northern Europe sowie des Head of M&A Europe bei der Auswertung von Statistiken in Bezug auf Schäden, Prämienentwicklung und Salesforce Kennzahlen. Was Sie mitbringen: Abgeschlossenes Studium der Rechts-, Ingenieur- oder Wirtschaftswissenschaften, Masterabschluss einer Universität oder Business School (MBA, LL.M., etc.) wünschenswert Mindestens erste Berufserfahrung im Bereich M&A Erste relevante Berufserfahrung im Versicherungswesen bzw. im Banking Exzellente Kommunikationsfähigkeiten sowie verhandlungssicheres Deutsch und Englisch (weitere Fremdsprachen von Vorteil) Teamfähigkeit und schnelle Auffassungsgabe sowie Entscheidungsfreude Hohe Belastbarkeit besonders bei der Arbeit unter Stress und Termindruck sowie hohes Durchsetzungsvermögen Gute Word-Kenntnisse, Grundkenntnisse bzgl. Excel- und PowerPoint Bereitschaft zur Reisetätigkeit Warum wir die beste Wahl sind: Wir bieten Ihnen vielfältige Aufgaben in einem digitalen und internationalen Umfeld mit flexiblen Arbeitszeiten. Wir arbeiten in flachen Hierarchien mit echten Gestaltungsmöglichkeiten. Teamarbeit steht bei uns an oberster Stelle. Regelmäßige Diversity & Inclusion-Veranstaltungen sowie Corporate Benefits und Fahrradleasing runden unsere Arbeitswelt ab. Zusatzleistungen wie 30 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr, arbeitsfreie Tage am 24.12. und 31.12., betriebliche Altersvorsorge und vermögenswirksame Leistungen sind für uns selbstverständlich. Wir haben Ihr Interesse geweckt? Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihren Lebenslauf direkt über unser Bewerbungsportal. Bei Fragen erreichen Sie Hanna Gamper (Talent Acquisition) unter +49 (0) 69 97113 234. #LI-AIG At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matter.

Through a robust stakeholder feedback loop and supported by consistent processes and leadership, we take pride in delivering responsive, fair and professional service with empathy and efficiency. As an Inland Marine Loss Adjuster you will be handling all aspects of Motor Truck Cargo, Vehicle Physical Damage, Car Hauler Liability, Commercial Property, Contractors Equipment, and Warehouse legal Liability claims. You will work alongside top experts, helping insureds manage and mitigate risks, often handling complex cases. Responsibilities include making coverage determinations, investigating losses, evaluating exposures, setting reserves, developing resolution strategies, managing outside surveyors, attorneys, and experts, negotiating with tow yards, working with underwriting on policy renewals, and promoting client relationships.

Position requires excellent negotiation skills, strong interpersonal and communication skills, knowledge of Inland Marine policy forms, Carmack amendment (preferred), and state adjuster licenses (preferred). Compensation includes base salary and potential bonuses. Benefits include health, wellbeing, financial security, and professional development. title: Field Risk Engineer
experience: Minimum one (1) year of highly protected risk, specialized industry and/or boiler and machinery field experience
educational_qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Science, Technology, Engineering or Mathematics
required_skill_set: Ability to perform inspections of buildings and structures
Ability to read construction drawings and diagrams
Possess at minimum of at least one year of driving experience with a valid U.S. driver’s license
Ability to perform physical activities (standing, walking, working at heights)
Ability to safely use ladders, scaffolds, lifts and access equipment
Ability to lift and carry materials up to 50 lbs
Ability to work outdoors and in hazardous locations
Ability to type at a computer for several hours
Ability to drive and operate a motor vehicle

additional_sections.content: Travel (~25% overnight) to conduct property loss prevention surveys at commercial facilities. Determine building construction and assess the impact of plastics on construction. Analyze water supplies and sprinkler system demands to determine water supply and sprinkler system adequacy. Identify occupancy hazards and assess the adequacy of their protection features. Identify and assess the impact of external exposures on a facility. Determine fire and natural catastrophe loss estimates for a facility. Conduct consultative visits at customer facilities. Conduct plan reviews. Witness fire protection equipment testing. Document survey results in a data-driven report writing application. Mentor and train lesser experienced staff.

Soft Skills: Highly motivated and works well without close supervision. The individual must be able to make decisions with and without established guidelines and to provide technical support to underwriters and clients. Ability to work closely with various Account Teams including Account Engineers and Underwriters. Ability to complete assignments on-time. Hard Skills: Professional Engineer or Certified Fire Protection Specialist Designation (desired but not required). Ability to perform inspections of buildings and structures, both visually and with instruments. Ability to read construction drawings, diagrams, printed materials. Possess at minimum of at least one year of driving experience with a valid U.S. driver’s license.

Ability to perform physical activities required for the position (standing, walking 8-10 hours a day, working at heights). Ability to understand and appreciate safety training specific to workplace and construction hazards. Ability to safely use ladders, scaffolds, lifts and access equipment. Ability to lift and carry materials and equipment up to 50 lbs. Ability to perform work outdoors. Ability to wear safety equipment (hard hat, safety shoes, glasses, hearing protection). Ability to work in hazardous locations. Ability to type at a computer for several hours. Ability to drive and operate a motor vehicle.

title: (AIG Sonpo) 延長保証 マネージャー
experience: 5年以上の損害保険業界での豊富な業務経験が望ましい
educational_qualifications: 学士号または同等の学位
required_skill_set: 調整能力およびコミュニケーション力
正確な日本語の読み書き能力
英語での業務に抵抗がない語学力

additional_sections.content: 損害保険ビジネスにおいて、アンダーライティング（保険引受業務）は中核かつ不可欠なものであり、アンダーライティング部門が行う意思決定は、保険会社の損益に直接影響します。延長保証保険のビジネスはAIGがグローバルで強みをもつ分野であり、今後の日本市場でのさらなる成長に向けて、アンダーライティング部門は延長保証保険の制度設計、販売促進、代理店教育、収支管理などへの貢献が期待されます。

延長保証のアンダーライティング部門のマネージャーとして、関連部門との連携の元、延長保証制度設計において、制度構築、引受判断、保険料設定等をリードして頂き、Head of Extended Warrantyへのレポートを行います。また、案件によってはGlobalのExtended Warrantyチームとの連携も必要となります。ブローカー、代理店等と協力しての販売促進をリードして頂きます。また、複雑な案件の場合は、顧客企業への直接的な説明を行う場合もあります。通常の商品管理業務（約款解釈の照会対応、募集文書チェック）およびプロジェクトとしての商品開発業務（約款改定、料率改定等）も職責の一つとなります。
内部関係先 (AIG組織内): AIG Globalの延長保証保険ライン（海外）、その他の商品ライン（主に国内）、営業部門、損害サービス部門、AIGグループにおける保証ビジネス管理会社。外部関係先 (AIG組織外): ディストリビューション・パートナー（代理店／ブローカーなど）。

最終学歴: 学士号または同等の学位。スキル/能力: 論理的思考力、調整能力およびコミュニケーション力、正確な日本語を読み書きできる能力、英語での業務に抵抗がない程度の語学力。業務経験: 損害保険業界における豊富な業務経験（5年以上望ましい）、新しいチャレンジと仕組みを作りに積極的に取り組める方、困難に直面しても粘り強く前向きに解決策を考えることができる方。尚可: 損害保険会社の商品業務部門における実務経験、損害保険の商品開発実務経験。

experience: Estudante de Administração, Economia, Direito entre outros cursos relacionados, com formação prevista a partir de 2027

additional_sections.content: O que é o seguro de crédito: é uma ferramenta de proteção financeira para empresas que vendem bens ou serviços a prazo (contas a receber), cobrindo prejuízos causados pela inadimplência (não pagamento ou atraso) de clientes, seja por falência (insolvência) ou mora. Ele protege o fluxo de caixa, permitindo expansão segura de vendas, tanto no mercado interno quanto em exportações. Apoiar a área de Seguro de Crédito no dia a dia do departamento, em atividades administrativas, em suporta ao fluxo de emissões de apólice, gestão e administração do seguro, em contato direto com clientes internos e externos; Preparar relatórios de acompanhamento e gestão de apólices; Acompanhar processos e interagir com a área Comercial, Produtos, Operações e Sinistros, assim como contato direto com corretores e clientes (indústrias, prestadores de serviços, bancos); Participar de treinamentos corporativos (técnicos e comportamentais), eventos de negócio e reuniões internas e externas; Desenvolvimento pessoal e profissional, dentro de uma ferramenta que move o mercado de Seguro de Crédito no Brasil e no mundo.

American International Group, Inc. (AIG) é uma líder global em soluções de seguros comerciais e pessoais; uma das redes de propriedade de risco mais abrangentes do mundo. A empresa valoriza a colaboração presencial, oferece benefícios abrangentes através do Total Rewards Program, promove uma cultura de inclusão e celebra a diversidade de talentos, origens, culturas, opiniões e objetivos.

title: Chief Security Information Officer, Malaysia
experience: 15+ years of full-time related work experience in information security management and/or related functions
educational_qualifications: Not specified
required_skill_set: Experience in financial or insurance industry
Security certifications such as SANS, ISC2, ISACA, ISO 27001

additional_sections.content: Responsible for providing security leadership, advice and counsel to executive management teams on security policy and practices; identifies exposures and threats and drives corrective plans based on risk. Implement the Company’s information security strategy across the business environments. Ensure the enterprise information security architecture and roadmaps are implemented and meet both business and information security objectives. Identify and understand key business processes, systems and specific security needs critical to Business Unit, and ensure they are incorporated into the overall cybersecurity strategy. Help develop business cases, secure leadership sponsorship, and drive adoption and implementation of security projects and programs. Support the business in its compliance to Malaysia Risk Management in Technology (RMiT) or any other relevant regulatory guidelines/standards issued by the local regulator. Help formulate and facilitate the effective implementation of relevant technology risk/information security standards required by the respective regulators (Eg RMiT Technology Risk Management Framework, and Cyber Resilience Framework) for the local entity. Ensure security projects and activities are prioritized based on risk to the business unit; work with leadership team to develop risk management program to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information owned, controlled or processed by the Company. Support anticipating, identifying, and deterring cyber-based attacks against AIG and its business operations, employees, and subsidiaries. Provide support to central team developing threat response and risk mitigation plans, including input into scenarios that involve invoking countermeasures and containment strategies. Ensure security issues are addressed with timely, appropriate responses to minimize the impact to the Company, or its assets, customers or reputation. Monitor and evaluate risk performance metrics on key security issues and programs, recommend corrective action programs as appropriate, and drive remediation items to completion. Actively contribute to the matters being discussed at the management committees (e.g., risk committee meetings, Board of Directors meeting) where information security is an invitee or a member, and where relevant, provide inputs of the matters under the information security purview. Remain current on constantly emerging Cybersecurity and geopolitical threats to ensure continual protection of the Company’s assets, information and reputation, ensuring the risk balance between risk posture and business agility.

Prior experience leading mid-size information security teams with a strong track record of success in complex environments. 15+ years of full-time related work experience in information security management and/or related functions (such as Technology Risk Management). At least 3 years holding a senior security leadership position at a company. Ability to communicate information security risks effectively in business terms to all levels of the organization. Experience in financial or insurance industry preferred, with an in-depth understanding of the relevant local regulatory requirements. Information security related certification such as SANS, ISC2, ISACA, ISO 27001 will be desirable. Trustworthy, proactive and takes leadership, with high standards of personal integrity. Typically, also possesses a background in technical IT roles such as security architecture, technology risk and governance or cyber security strategy - with an overarching information security oversight. Knowledge of security metrics and Key Security Risk indicators and how to implement them. Ability to work locally without need for visa/work permit sponsorship.

additional_sections.content: #LI- AIG Start your career as Property Analyst Location: United Arab Emirates, Dubai AIG offers its customers a wide range of tailored insurance solutions and is one of the largest international insurance companies in the world. We are 36,000+ colleagues providing services in approximately 70 countries and jurisdictions. AIG’s Underwriters embody our tradition of innovation, by bringing to market ground-breaking insurance solutions for our changing world. AIG’s underwriters help to find insurance solutions for risks from a range of areas including Financial Lines, Property, Casualty, Specialty Lines, Cyber, Multinational Clients and High Net Worth individuals. Your start @ AIG Our Early Career Program is aimed at graduates from all disciplines who are looking to integrate a diverse international community and to start a career that nurtures their potential. The Early Career Program is for those who enjoy exchanges and negotiations and who could see themselves interacting with brokers and customers internationally. The goal of our program is to help you build an exciting and fulfilling career at AIG. You will have the chance to join one of our key lines: Financial Lines, Property, Casualty, Multinational, Package or Claims. In addition to the training on-the-job, you will participate in the AIG Academy, a two-year structured development program designed to support and accelerate your strengths and interests. To help you succeed, we make sure you receive above-average remuneration, regardless of your degree. You will be accompanied by a mentor, and you will have the chance to participate in global workshops, master classes, as well as networking events to get to know colleagues from the AIG worldwide. You will receive a permanent employment contract. What you can expect: Support and independent review of risks Preparation of customized offers for national and international customers High-level of personal responsibility and entrepreneurial work in a helpful and committed team English-speaking communication with local and foreign brokers, customers, and colleagues of the company's own international network Collaboration on interdepartmental and strategic projects Steep learning curve and a variety of development opportunities What you bring: Bachelor's or Master's degree, e.g. in Business Administration, Economics, Information Technology, Law, Engineering, Actuarial Sciences or Risk Management, graduated in 2025 or 2026. Initial work experience through internships is an advantage, ideally in the insurance sector Motivated by innovation and novelty Readiness to be on a steep learning curve and agile comprehension skills Ability to work in a team, as well as willingness to make decisions Very good English language skills MS Office skills We’re proud to offer a range of employee benefits and resources that help you protect what matters most – your health care, savings, financial protection, and life quality. Our global mental health and wellness days off provide all colleagues with an opportunity to focus on their wellbeing. We offer you diverse, exciting tasks in a digital and international environment with flexible working hours and smart working. We work in flat hierarchies with real opportunities for creativity. Teamwork is our top priority. Interested? Become a part of our team! Start dates: July 01st, 2026 What you’ll need to know: At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why team members come together in the office four days per week. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Diversity and inclusion are central components of our corporate culture and crucial to the success of our company today and in the future. They form the foundation, give room to creativity, make us more innovative, and contribute to growth and profitability. We promote the development of our employees with numerous programs and initiatives. After all, our employees should not only be respected as individuals, but also valued for their unique perspectives. Learn more about AIG's values here.

title: Project Manager
company_name: AIG SEGUROS MEXICO S.A. DE C.V.
experience: Not specified
educational_qualifications: Not specified

additional_sections.content: American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a leading global insurance organization. Building on 100 years of experience, today AIG member companies provide a wide range of property casualty insurance, life insurance, retirement solutions, and other financial services to customers in more than 80 countries and jurisdictions.

This role sits at the center of Operations transformation and execution. You will partner directly with the COO and senior leaders to turn strategic priorities into actionable, measurable initiatives that improve scalability, service quality, and cost efficiency. You will lead complex, cross-functional programs that span multiple operational areas (CPS, Collections, Call Center and external partners), ensuring alignment between business needs, technology capabilities, and vendor performance.

Operations is a high-volume, SLA-driven environment where speed, quality, and cost must be balanced daily. Projects typically involve multiple stakeholders (Operations, IT, Finance, Vendors, Compliance). Many initiatives include outsourcing/BPO transitions and operating model redesign. Executive exposure is frequent; communication must be concise, data-driven, and decision-oriented. Delivery expectations are high — this role owns outcomes, not just coordination. Change management and stakeholder alignment are as critical as technical execution. Impact is measured through tangible operational and financial results.

Behavioral competencies include communication skills, customer orientation, adaptability, results-driven mindset, process orientation, change leadership, collaboration, customer-centricity, analytical skills, resilience, ownership mindset. Functional competencies include ownership & accountability, structured & analytical thinking, influencing & stakeholder management, results orientation, change leadership & adaptability, executive communication, collaboration & team enablement.

AIG offers a range of employee benefits including health care, savings, financial protection, wellbeing, leaves for personal, health, family, military needs, and a 'Giving Back' program. They also provide learning opportunities, tuition reimbursement, and a culture of inclusion and diversity.

AIG values in-person collaboration, fosters a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities, and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). They are committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion, providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodations for employees with disabilities.

title: Claims Adjuster - Marine
company_name: AIG Europe S.A. (U.K. Branch) & American International Group UK Ltd
experience: Experience and knowledge in handling: Cargo First Party, Cargo Third Party, Shippers Interest claims. All areas not essential. Policy language skills enabling accurate and consistent policy wording interpretation. Experience of using ECF helpful but not essential. A working knowledge of legal / regulatory and litigation / procedural requirements for their line of business. Experience in effectively following up on recommendations from technical claims audits and continuous claim handling improvement.

additional_sections.content: Your future team Picture yourself building a successful career at AIG working alongside other talented, highly motivated individuals committed to reaching our goal of becoming the world’s leading insurance provider. We believe that our success depends on building a world-class team. As a Marine Claims Adjuster, you will handle marine claims of varying complexity and value. The handling will start from first notification through policy & legal liability assessment, quantum analysis to timely and accurate resolution, whilst mitigating indemnity and expense exposure. You will communicate developments and outcome as necessary to all key stakeholders (internal and external) and provide a high standard of customer service while adhering to claims handling best practices and legal, regulatory and compliance requirements.

Managing key claims handling enquiries; coverage determination, quantum analysis and legal liability assessment, where appropriate within authority limits and providing a high standard of customer service. Ability to resolve issues that require good knowledge and judgement and will escalate more complex issues. Ensuring effective vendor and litigation management on Complex claims within a personal allocation. Broader internal / external stakeholder communication where required including with underwriters, clients, brokers and surveyors. Strive for continuous improvement on claim file handling with feedback and support through the Quality Assurance Review processes. Contribute to maintenance of best practice procedures for (LoB) Complex claims, consistent with global best practice. Demonstrate a high standard of technical claims competence. Timely, accurate and customer focused claim resolution, minimising indemnity exposure and mitigating vendor and legal expense. Effective communication of key Complex claims and (LoB) portfolio messages to internal stakeholders. Financial control through consistent reserving and other financial transaction discipline. Accurate and consistent policy interpretation. Supporting the wider team in claims performance orientated tasks and reporting as required. Supporting the wider team in ad-hoc projects as required.

Experience and knowledge in handling: Cargo First Party, Cargo Third Party, Shippers Interest claims. All areas not essential. Policy language skills enabling accurate and consistent policy wording interpretation. Experience of using ECF helpful but not essential. A working knowledge of legal / regulatory and litigation / procedural requirements for their line of business. Experience in effectively following up on recommendations from technical claims audits and continuous claim handling improvement.

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters. At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world. American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become. Welcome to a culture of inclusion. We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. title: Operational Account Manager
experience: Experience in the HVAC and/or Warranty industry is preferred.
required_skill_set: Ability to manage large escalations and solve complex issues

additional_sections.content: At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as an HVAC Operational Account Manager to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. Make your mark in the HVAC Business unit AIG Warranty delivers a full range of service solutions including warranty management administration, extended service programs, customer service support, service network management, claims processing services, and service contract underwriting. With over 20 years of experience, we are one of the warranty and service industry's leading providers, which is why many of the world's largest and most respected brands choose AIG. We deliver flexible and innovative service solutions to our OEM and retail partners, focusing on the HVACR industry. The HVAC Operational Account Manager will manage processes for assigned HVAC accounts while striving to enhance efficiencies, along with serving as a point of contact to implement operational aspects for new programs and processes. The primary focus for the position is process optimization for assigned HVAC Accounts and will report to the Client Operations AVP. Work with clients, Operations Process Director and Client Operations AVP to implement and monitor operational processes, as well as create best practices. Establish regular cadence for reporting on assigned account activities and processes. Build relationships within the HVAC industry and be a trusted advisor. Work closely with project team and various departments on implementation of operational processes for new and existing accounts. Create and maintain standard operating procedures. Research data issues and determine process to correct with input from client, internal departments, and other stakeholders. Attend and assist with preparing data for Quarterly Business Review clients and present in a highly professional manner to executives and key stakeholders, both internal and external. Translate the needs of clients and customers into operational plans. Performs other duties as required to support AIG and account growth objectives and strategic initiatives.

Gestion d’un portefeuille de sinistres Responsabilité Civile Entreprises sur des comptes nationaux et multinationaux. Assurer le pilotage du dossier en coordination avec les courtiers et assurés. Analyser les garanties du contrat. Déterminer les responsabilités. Missionner les experts, les avocats et les médecins. Négocier le règlement des sinistres à l’amiable ou au judiciaire. Effectuer un contrôle financier par le biais de révisions régulières et de réserves ajustées. Participer à des réunions avec nos conseils, des assurés et des courtiers. Gérer des sinistres pour le compte des bureaux étrangers dans le cadre de programmes internationaux. Collaborer avec le département souscription RC. Faire remonter l’information importante sur les sinistres.

additional_sections.content: This HR Professional role will be an ideal opportunity to develop your experience and expertise in human resources. Make your mark in Human Resources At AIG, people are our most valued asset, the primary source of our competitive advantage and the foundation of everything we do. The HR team partners with business leaders to recruit, develop and retain top talent. We position our colleagues to reach their full potential by providing them with the tools and opportunities to develop their career while maintaining a work/life balance. With a focus on Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) efforts, one of AIG and HR’s highest priorities, HR fosters a sense of belonging and encourages our colleagues to be themselves in the workplace. How you will create an impact: Oversee end-to-end employee lifecycle processes including onboarding, probationary reviews, contract administration, and termination procedures, ensuring compliance with local labor regulations and company policies. Provide support to the talent acquisition team for early career recruitment, implement best practices in sourcing and candidate engagement. Execute and coordinate HR initiatives, employee engagement programs to foster an inclusion organizational culture. Organize and host new hires' orientation. Be responsible for preparing the HR internal communications, including holiday announcements, wellness programs, and organizational updates to ensure consistent HR messaging across the company. Promote our Employer Value Proposition, amplify employment branding initiatives, and continuously elevate the end-to-end employee experience. Participate in Ad-hoc tasks/projects assigned by managers.

title: Claims Process Analyst - Service Programs
experience: Proven experience in a process analyst, operations analyst, or business analyst role (experience in insurance or claims is preferred).
required_skill_set: Advanced proficiency in Excel, including use of pivot tables, formulas, and VBA/macros
Familiarity with Power Automate or other data automation tools
Ability to communicate clearly with technical and non-technical stakeholders
Comfortable working with large datasets and ensuring data quality
Self-motivated and able to work under pressure

additional_sections.content: At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Claims Process Analyst to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. Make your mark in the Claims Department. At AIG, our claims teams are at the heart of delivering on our promise to customers—providing fair, efficient, and timely claims handling across a broad range of insurance products, including Personal Lines, Property, Casualty, Specialty Lines, Cyber, Multinational Clients, and High Net Worth individuals. We are reimagining the claims experience by streamlining processes, harnessing technology, and evolving our approach to better support our customers when they need us most. AIG empowers claims professionals with the tools, training, and career development opportunities to grow, innovate, and make a meaningful impact—both for our customers and our business. How you will create an impact: Collaborate with claims team and relevant stakeholders to understand operational pain points, particularly related to the handling of bordereaux and bulk claims. Analyze existing end-to-end claims processes, workflows, and data handling practices to identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and improvement opportunities. Design and implement solutions that streamline manual processes, including automation using Excel macros, formulas, or Power Automate. Standardize the way bordereaux are reviewed, reconciled, and approved for payment, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and compliance with AIG’s claims handling guidelines, ensuring timely resolution. Document process changes and provide training to claims team. Support the implementation of best practices and continuous improvement initiatives across the team.

Proven experience in a process analyst, operations analyst, or business analyst role (experience in insurance or claims is preferred). Advanced proficiency in Excel, including use of pivot tables, formulas, and VBA/macros. Familiarity with Power Automate or other data automation tools is highly desirable. Strong problem-solving skills, with a structured and analytical approach. Ability to communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Comfortable working with large datasets and ensuring data quality. Self-motivated and able to work under pressure with a high level of responsibility.
