additional_sections.content: AIG is a market leader in Cyber Insurance. Our Cyber portfolio has experienced significant growth in the last 24 months in terms of both GWP and policy count. We see the continuation of that growth as a key long-term business objective and are seeking the right individual to play a critical role in contributing to both the retention and growth of our UK Cyber and Tech PI portfolio of business. The role will be supported by the Financial Lines underwriting team including close and regular contact with our cyber colleagues based in London. Regular training on technical developments, current trends and claims insights will be provided across our international cyber team. You will use your existing knowledge along with the training provided to contribute to the cyber and Tech PI underwriting strategy across our portfolio. This will range from detailed underwriting of large UK based clients to volume based digital solutions for SME clients. This role can be based in any of our regional offices in Manchester, Glasgow or Birmingham.

Meet individual underwriting expectations to help team achieve budgeted financial objectives. Underwrite within agreed decision-making standards, limitations, and underwriting authority. Assess desirability of risk through analysis of financial reports and accounts; regular contact with broker; thorough review of proposal; and review of claims history and general company information. Use designated Underwriting system(s) to streamline underwriting and information-tracking approach across Profit Centre. Input required data consistently and accurately. Provide Management information including, but not limited to, submissions, bind rate, average premium, and total premium as required. Provide excellent service to brokers through consistent, timely, and proactive approach to underwriting. Exhibit sales orientated behaviors, defined by Management as: motivated, tenacious, focused, technically skilled, proactive, and accountable. Generate new business by building on existing relationships and expanding broker network, attending broker events, market meetings, client 1 to 1’s and building new relationships across the market. Support and contribute to the Cyber & tech PI underwriting strategy within the UK regions portfolio. Maintain key renewal accounts at greater than 85% retention. Ensure all brokers and clients are treated fairly. Ensure contract certainty. Conduct self-audit as required. Ensure compliance with key regulatory and risk management responsibilities.

Underwriting experience is preferred. The ability to analyze financial statements. Strong communication, networking and relationship building skills. Good organizational and time management skills. Customer-centric approach. Customer and Sales orientated behaviors: motivated, tenacious, focused, technically skilled, proactive, and accountable.

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters. At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world. American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become. With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. Outcome focused, self-motivated, flexible and enthusiastic
Strong interpersonal skills with emphasis on collaboration and teamwork
Proficient working knowledge of Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Outlook

additional_sections.content: Lexington has an opportunity for a Senior Production Specialist on the U.S. Builders Risk team specializing in underwriting primary, quota share and excess construction risk opportunities. The Senior Production Specialist is expected to work effectively in an independent manner to successfully manage his/her book of business of new and renewal production along with executing the profitability strategy for the territory’s portfolio. A Senior Production Specialist must be able to illustrate sufficient technical analysis and underwriting acumen combined with marketing and sales skills to meet underwriting goals (i.e., achieve growth targets and maintain overall profitability). In addition, the ability to coach, train and develop current staff to their fullest potential is required.

As a senior underwriter on the builder's risk team, you are respected for your technical expertise and/or ability to oversee complex programs and processes. You balance the needs of multiple stakeholders, making sound decisions using data, analysis, experience, and judgment, along with a risk mindset. Ultimately, you take ownership for key outcomes. Produce, underwrite, manage and service a profitable book of business within the team structure. Utilize underwriter authority in accordance with the published underwriting guidelines. Handle a portfolio of Master Builders Risk program renewals and new single project business made up of complex risks. Ability to seek out and partner with internal / external stakeholders in securing new profitable business. Manage and develop effective producer and client relationships throughout the U.S. Work alongside business development colleagues to effectively maximize our distribution partner network. Effectively negotiate and secure policy terms that align with the company directives, especially utilizing new product and service offerings to bring value to the client. Develop and manage underwriting strategies for both renewals and prospects within his/her portfolio of accounts, including knowledge of the current penetration by product, and specific plans to appropriately manage capacity. Provide direction and support to less experienced underwriters on new and renewal business. Proactively foster and develop positive relationships with key customers. Execute established strategy and work collaboratively with other aligned business functions including claims, actuarial, engineering, operations, analytics, legal and marketing. Make internal and external presentations as assigned and deemed necessary.

Minimum of 7 years underwriting experience in the Builders Risk or Property. 4-year college / university Bachelor's Degree. Extensive knowledge of builder’s risk and property forms and wordings. Strong relationships with retail and / or wholesale brokerage partners. Knowledge of the builder’s risk market, portfolio management, acquisition and distribution. Ability to work in a fast-paced environment making quick decisions while adhering to a sound underwriting discipline. Outcome focused, self-motivated, flexible and enthusiastic. Strong interpersonal skills with emphasis on collaboration and teamwork. Proficient working knowledge of Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Outlook is required.

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. We offer a range of competitive benefits, a summary of which can be viewed here: US Benefits Overview. We are committed to creating a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). We provide equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories. Reasonable accommodations are available for applicants with disabilities.

title: Expert en sinistres bilingue, Pertes Majeurs, Responsabilité Civile / Bilingual Major Loss Adjuster, Casualty Claims
experience: Minimum 10 years in handling commercial liability claims
educational_qualifications: College/University degree or equivalent experience

additional_sections.content: Expert en sinistres bilingue, Pertes Majeurs, Responsabilité Civile Chez AIG, nous réinventons la façon dont nous aidons nos clients à gérer les risques. Rejoignez-nous en tant qu'expert en sinistres, pertes majeurs en responsabilité civile et participez à cette transformation. C'est l'occasion de développer vos compétences et votre expérience au sein d'une équipe précieuse. Faites votre marque dans le domaine des réclamations Nos équipes de gestion des sinistres sont reconnues pour leur expertise et leur capacité à résoudre les problèmes de nos clients. Elles fournissent une expertise technique rigoureuse et constante et mettent de l’avant une approche différenciée, visant à offrir une expérience de gestion des sinistres de calibre mondial. Grâce à un système rigoureux de rétroaction, à des processus bien établis et à un leadership constant, nous sommes fiers de fournir un service réactif, équitable et professionnel, guidé par l’empathie et l’efficacité. Comment vous créerez un impact Expert en sinistres, pertes majeures est responsable du traitement de divers types de réclamations complexes en assurance responsabilité civile commerciale, notamment les recours collectifs, les réclamations pour dommages matériels importants, les réclamations liées aux rappels de produits et autres réclamations en responsabilité civile générale. Il doit exercer ses fonctions conformément à toutes les directives opérationnelles aux meilleures pratiques en matière de gestion des sinistres. Ses responsabilités incluent notamment l’analyse et la détermination de la couverture applicable, la rédaction des lettres de réserve de droits et de négation, la participation aux médiations et aux conférences de règlement, ainsi que la coordination des experts indépendants et avocats externe dans le cadre de la négociation et du règlement des réclamations. Les réclamations concernent principalement le Canada, avec certains sinistres aux États-Unis et à l'international. Vos Responsabilités : Analyser les termes et la condition des polices d'assurance afin de déterminer la couverture. Rédiger des lettres de négation et/ou de réserve de droits et les communiquer aux assurés, courtiers et autres parties concernées. Donner des instructions claires aux avocats externes afin d’assurer un règlement approprié des dossiers. S'assurer que les avocats externes respectent les lignes directrices en matière de litiges. Participer aux processus de médiation/conférence de règlement afin de parvenir à un règlement du sinistre. Procéder à des négociations de règlement direct lorsque les circonstances le permettent. Effectuer les paiements dans les délais prescrits. Appliquer les meilleures pratiques, notamment en matière de révision des réserves, de tenue de notes, de demandes de paiement, et de gestion de l’agenda. Informer la direction de toutes les plaintes, des sinistres importants, des problèmes de couverture, etc. Ce dont vous aurez besoin pour réussir : Détient une licence commerciale AMF valide La maîtrise de l'anglais et du français (verbal et écrit) est essentielle, car une partie des dossiers traités auront lieu à l'extérieur du Québec ou avec une clientèle anglophone. Minimum de 10 ans d'expérience dans le traitement des sinistres en assurance responsabilité civile commerciale Formation collégiale ou universitaire ou expérience équivalente dans le secteur Capacité de prendre des décisions et de donner des directives aux experts en sinistres indépendant et/ou aux avocats Capacité d'apprentissage rapide et excellentes aptitudes à la communication et à la négociation Excellentes aptitudes relationnelles Maîtrise des outils informatiques pour la gestion informatisée des dossiers de sinistres Déplacements : Environ 10 % pour assister à des médiations.

title: (AIG Sonpo) パーソナルラインズ分析部 データアナリスト・サイエンティスト
experience: Not mandatory but preferred if experienced in data analysis, programming, or related fields.
educational_qualifications: 学士号または同等の学位。
required_skill_set: プログラミング作成業務

additional_sections.content: 各種統計データベース（DWHおよび料率DBなど）を利用し、各部署（主に自動車保険部・個人火災保険部・個人旅行保険担当）が求めるデータの提供や分析を通じて、会社の成長と収益向上に貢献する。売上高、損害率、収益性分析などの主要業績評価指標（KPI）を分析し、会社の収益性の高い成長に貢献します。主に自動車保険部門と個人火災部門を中心とした部門横断的なチームと連携し、様々なプロジェクトにデータと分析のサポートを提供し、プロジェクトのメリットを迅速に実現し、ビジネス価値を高めます。

各部署（主に自動車保険部、個人火災保険部・個人旅行保険担当）の各メンバーと協力し、部門内で遂行する各種プロジェクトに積極的にデータ支援として参加することによって、そのプロジェクトによる効果を早期に会社に還元する。各種データ分析（損害率分析や収益分析）を実施、または各部署（主に自動車保険部・個人火災保険部）の各種データ分析を支援することで、会社の成長と収益向上に貢献する。各分野の専門家としてチームを率い、部門メンバーを含む社内外の関係者と連携することで、ビジネス成果の向上と組織目標との整合性を確保する。
【内部】個人セグメント保険部門（主に自動車保険部・個人火災保険部）、クレーム部門、ディストリビューション部門、アクチュアリ部門、その他データを必要とする部門。【外部】日本損害保険料率算出機構（GIRO）、日本損害保険協会。

【学歴】学士号または同等の学位。【資格】データ処理を行うためのプログラミング作成業務や数値を扱う業務に興味を持っていること。特にデータサイエンスと機械学習にも興味を持ち、Pythonプログラミングなどの経験があればなお望ましい。各種保険データに対して、データを伴う分析業務に興味を持っていること。複雑さに対処する能力があり、協調性があること。積極的に仕事を引き受けられる自発性があること。【経験】必須ではないが上記経験や素養があればなお望ましい。

additional_sections.content: Promote a customer-focused environment. Ensure all regional Producing Office (PO), Issuing Office (IO) and network partners (NP) service expectations and Service Level Agreement (SLA’s) are met or exceeded in accordance with mandated processes and operating procedures. Ensure continuous velocity of Multinational requests across the Region to directly enhance our ability to deliver a superior experience, improve effectiveness and enhance Multinational workforce efficiency.

jobPostingInfo.title: Regional Multinational Service Delivery Manager – Latin America and the Caribbean (LAC)
jobPostingInfo.jobDescription:
Regional Multinational Service Delivery Manager – Latin America and the Caribbean (LAC)

At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as the Regional Multinational Service Delivery Manager - Latin America and the Caribbean (LAC) to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team.


Who we are:
American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a leading global insurance organization. Building on 100 years of experience, today AIG member companies provide a wide range of property casualty insurance, life insurance, retirement solutions, and other financial services to customers in more than 80 countries and jurisdictions. These diverse offerings include products and services that help businesses and individuals protect their assets, manage risks and provide for retirement security.

Get to know the business 
General Insurance is a leading provider of insurance products and services – focused on helping to manage risk for commercial and personal customers. We offer a broad range of products through a diversified, multi-channel distribution network, including one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks.

Multinational
AIG’s Multinational business is an exciting area within General Insurance that will challenge you every day to explore new opportunities for our clients worldwide. As a trusted advisor, you will tailor multinational insurance solutions for our global clients, empowering them to operate across borders with confidence and consistency. Partner with a dynamic, global team of experts, including underwriters, risk engineers, and analytics and claims professionals, to design, implement and service multinational insurance programs. This includes issuing timely policies, providing regulatory advice, sharing local knowledge, supporting efficient money movement capabilities and deploying large fronted limits. AIG Multinational professionals have the diverse skills and expertise necessary to deliver customized, compliant and seamless solutions that support our clients’ risk management objectives. With in-depth knowledge of local markets, practices and regulations, Multinational provides world-class insurance solutions and helps clients successfully navigate their cross-border risks, wherever in the world their businesses take them.

How you will create an impact
The Regional Multinational (MN) Service Delivery Manager will work to implement the MN Business Model, agreed MN Organizational Alignment, Resource & Efficiency Plans and globally consistent MN processes, roles & responsibilities, procedures, controls, performance metrics throughout all countries / territories within assigned region and across the AIG Network (including non AIG countries), partnering with relevant stakeholders such as in country local leadership teams, Fac desk underwriters, MN Operations & Training, Quality, Risk & Compliance colleagues to meet targeted standards in terms of quality, customer service, compliance, and timeliness, all of which underpin AIG’s Multinational Value Proposition. The position will be responsible to coordinate servicing efforts for PO´s and IO´s but also Network partner territories - including complicated negotiations, regulations, relationships and agreements and external parties like local reinsurers in territories such as Argentina, Venezuela, Chile, Colombia, etc. In addition to the service delivery discipline the role will require development of thought leadership strategies across all countries such as podcasts, videos, and social media updates to embrace the MN strategy “Telling the MN story”. The Regional Multinational Service Delivery Manager, will report directly to the Head of Multinational (LAC) Detailed Responsibilities: Promote a customer-focused environment. Ensure all regional Producing Office (PO), Issuing Office (IO) and network partners (NP) service expectations and Service Level Agreement (SLA’s

title: Underwriter, Commercial Property
company_name: AIG Europe S.A. (U.K. Branch) & American International Group UK Ltd
experience: Property underwriting experience and strong technical insurance knowledge
educational_qualifications: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent, ACII is an advantage
required_skill_set: Relationship building with brokers and clients

additional_sections.content: Make your mark in Underwriting AIG underwriting teams help to find insurance solutions in areas including Financial Lines, Property, Casualty, Specialty Lines, Cyber, Multinational Clients, and High Net Worth individuals. We are reimagining how we help customers to manage risk, transforming our operating model, and reshaping our role responsibilities and career pathways. The goal is to unlock the full potential in each colleague – empowering our people to grow as insurance professionals and add more value to our customers and AIG. About the role As an Underwriter in the Major Accounts Property team you will work with some of AIG’s most high-profile clients with a focus on those with total insurable assets greater than $1bn. You will partner directly with Brokers and Clients helping them to identify risks, reduce exposure and ensure they have access to AIG’s core value proposition. How you will create an impact Contribute to the strategic development of our Major Accounts portfolio; build visibility with brokers and clients; drive the client service process. Develop broker relationships establishing accretive pipelines, and cross selling opportunities across the AIG network. Work cross functionally with other areas in AIG to achieve corporate objectives. Work within agreed decision making standards, limitations, authorities and underwriting guidelines. Achieve satisfactory ratings on all underwriting quality reviews. Ensure all corporate service standards are maintained to both brokers & internal colleagues. Ability to prioritise workload and to use own initiative for both internal and external issues. Maintain and develop strong broker and client relationships. Support the culture of continuous improvement in all areas including improving processes to make the business more efficient and creating an environment where people are encouraged to take initiative and responsibility.

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters. At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world. American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become. Welcome to a culture of inclusion. We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories. AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com.

Scrap_json.jobDescription:
Client Director 

Join us as a Client Director to play a highly influential role in protecting our clients and growing our business.  

Our Client and Broker Engagement (CBE) team manages AIG’s major client relationships across all product lines, as well as driving new business activity. The team serves major clients and broker partners through an elevated, personalized experience – our client-centric framework drives a consistent experience and best practice, while allowing flexibility to meet the client’s needs.

The team serves major clients and broker partners through an elevated, personalized experience – our client-centric framework drives a consistent experience and best practice, while allowing flexibility to meet the client’s needs. 

What you need to know: 

AIG General Insurance has an exciting opportunity for a Client Director in Chicago. The responsibilities will involve management of key client and broker relationships in the area. A successful candidate will have proven ability to align and influence internal and external stakeholders, is an effective communicator, has the ability to build commitment, and is tolerant of ambiguity. Key attributes for the role include leadership, creativity, collaboration, strong influential skills, presence, vision and broad insurance knowledge. 

How you will create an impact: 

Client Engagement: 

  • Build valuable client relationships with key personnel, including C-suite executives 

  • Serve as the senior AIG point of contact for key client partners 

  • Formulate Strategic Action Plans with clients in order to identify critical client focus areas, existing AIG alignment and forward looking opportunities for increased partnership 

  • Identify and maintain regular collaborative dialogue with key business influencers at major brokerage operations   

Internal Management: 

  • Champion new and existing business initiatives working with the Broker Marketing Executive, Broker Marketing Leader, Broker Engagement and Underwriting Business Partners.

  • Collaborate with Zonal leadership to ensure Client Centric Focus is integrated into all Zonal sales and business planning 

  • Develop and implement collaborative marketing strategies to achieve business production and performance goals for the Zone 

  • Manage account and product portfolio across Distribution and Profit Center resources 

  • Drive the AIG value proposition and ensure that clients are aware of the array of products, services and solutions (enterprise-wide) available and enhance exposure to AIG 

  • Develop plans to improve portfolio and account performance, enhance service and strengthen relationships with major account clients 

  • Deliver results in new business production and cross selling from existing clients in line with set objectives 

  • Meet or exceed client retention and premium retention goals for Major Account Segment in the Zone 

What you'll need to succeed: 

  • 10+ years of experience 

  • Proven client relationship / management experience 

  • Experience in P&C Insurance 

  • Ability to build relationships, internally and externally 

  • Ability to influence brokers, clients, peers, senior executives and staff 

  • Ability to manage complex global insurance relationships 

  • Strong financial and accounting skills to effectively engage at the senior management level 

  • Strong problem solving abilities.    

For positions based in Illinois, the base salary range is $125,000 to $173,000 and the position is eligible for a bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable incentive plan. In addition, we’re proud to offer a range of competitive benefits, a summary of which can be viewed here: :  2026 Benefits Overview

#LI-AIG

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike.

At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family.

Building on 100 years of experience, today AIG member companies provide a wide range of property casualty insurance, life insurance, retirement solutions, and other financial services to customers in more than 80 countries and jurisdictions. These diverse offerings include products and services that help businesses and individuals protect their assets, manage risks and provide for retirement security.

The Early Career Program is aimed at graduates from all disciplines who are looking to integrate a diverse international community and to start a career that nurtures their potential. The program includes on-the-job training, participation in the AIG Academy, mentorship, global workshops, master classes, networking events, and a permanent employment contract.

Underwrite new business and manage a portfolio in accordance with the underwriting policy, monitor existing portfolio against market trends, work in coordination with technical, sales, administrative, marketing, and claims teams, build and maintain relationships with broker partners, participate in meetings and technical training, and participate in new product development initiatives.

Eager to learn, passionate about working in a dynamic international environment, a team player with an open and positive mindset, interested in data science, customer relations, and innovation, with previous work/internship experience, strong academic and extracurricular achievement, and fluent in English and Dutch with a good basis of French.
Competitive benefit package, global career opportunities, extensive training and personal development programs, flexible working hours, smart working, and a culture of inclusion and belonging.

title: Souscripteur principal bilingue en assurance-crédit commercial / Bilingual Senior Underwriter, Trade Credit
experience: 2-3 years in underwriting, experience in insurance required
educational_qualifications: MBA or third cycle training (preferred)
required_skill_set: Fluency in English and French (verbal and written)
Experience in credit or financial analysis
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint), S&P Cap IQ, Salesforce

additional_sections.content: Chez AIG, nous réimaginons constamment notre façon d’aider nos clients à gérer les risques. Joignez-vous à nous pour participer activement à cette transformation en tant que souscripteur exécutif, assurance responsabilité de gestion d’entreprise. C’est l’occasion idéale de perfectionner vos compétences et d’approfondir votre expérience en tant que membre important de l’équipe. Les équipes de souscription d’AIG aident à trouver des solutions d’assurance dans des secteurs tels que les produits financiers, l’assurance des biens, l’assurance responsabilité, les risques spécialisés, l’assurance contre les cyber-risques, les clients multinationaux et les produits d’assurance pour les particuliers fortunés. Nous réimaginons constamment notre façon d’aider nos clients à gérer les risques, en transformant notre modèle opérationnel et en restructurant nos responsabilités et nos parcours professionnels. Nous visons à réaliser le plein potentiel de chaque collègue - en donnant à nos collaborateurs les moyens de se perfectionner en tant que professionnels de l’assurance et d’offrir une plus grande valeur à nos clients et à AIG.

Développer des relations solides avec les courtiers et les clients afin de développer le portefeuille d'assurance-crédit de l'assureur. Souscrire de nouvelles polices d'assurance-crédit et renouveler les polices existantes, et négocier les termes et conditions des contrats. Analyser les crédits des entreprises nationales et internationales, évaluer les informations financières et recommander ou approuver les limites de crédit et les conditions appropriées. Gérer et assurer un service de qualité pour le portefeuille existant d'assurance-crédit. Mener des actions de marketing et de vente auprès des courtiers et des assurés afin d'accroître le volume des demandes. Participer à divers projets de souscription et de marketing pour améliorer les résultats globaux de l'entreprise. Respecter les directives et les objectifs opérationnels et d'audit de la direction. Soutenir l'équipe de souscription et les souscripteurs seniors. Appuyer la direction dans la production de rapports et la réalisation de projets, selon les besoins.

La maîtrise de l'anglais et du français (verbal et écrit) est essentielle, car une partie importante de la souscription gérée par l'employé aura lieu à l'extérieur du Québec. title: Senior Underwriter - Trade Credit - Multinational & Strategic
experience: Mid-Senior level
educational_qualifications: Not specified

additional_sections.content: Join us as a Senior Underwriter – Trade Credit to grow your career at the forefront of Trade Credit insurance. At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Senior Underwriter focusing on strategic and multinational accounts to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. The successful candidate will be responsible for managing a portfolio of excess of loss (XOL) clients and supporting our new business Underwriters on our ambitious growth targets. As a dual pen underwriter, the successful candidate will be responsible for being the key point of contact for a number of XOL clients on their credit insurance arrangements. This will include setting buyer credit limits on the portfolio and also negotiating commercial terms with the client at renewal also. The role will also include development of relationships with brokers and clients to ensure successful renewals and growth of the AIG book of business in the UK.

Trade Credit Insurance allows clients to trade with confidence with their customers, safe in the knowledge they will get paid for the goods they sell on credit. The product can form part of a company’s risk management and also potentially provide capital relief on borrowing costs. Within the UK Strategic & Multinational team we are typically targeting the world’s largest companies to help them with their credit exposures. Expect to work with the world’s biggest and most successful names on global insurance programs. The successful candidate will become the key contact for a portfolio of large credit insurance clients, helping them to review their customer’s credit worthiness.

Hold a ‘dual-pen’ authority – forming a single point of contact for a portfolio of clients. Work on new business development in partnership with our dedicated new business Underwriters and global Multinational leadership team. Assessing specific counterparts and their financial statements to set agreed credit limits. Negotiating commercial terms with our customers at year end renewals and during the mid-point of policies where appropriate. Develop and maintain a strong relationship with key broking partners. Working with clients to improve their credit management and steer them clear of bad debts. Underwriting to an agreed acceptable loss ratio. Being up to date with sector, economic and political risk trends. Work with the head of Strategic & Multinational in the UK on budgeting and reporting. Participate in the AIG Global Solutions team supporting the establishment of the Trade Credit Multinational AGS brans as a leading XOL market and multinational program Underwriter. The UK Trade Credit team is on a mission of continuous improvement of our teams, processes and service offered to clients. Expect to work on a personal development pathway to improve your skillset.

A good understanding of financial assessment including ability to understand profit and loss, balance sheet and cash flow analysis. Proven business acumen and successful commercial career experiences with multinational programs, MN companies and global brokers. Good understanding of Trade Credit, wordings, processes and documentation. Good communicator with presentation, communication and negotiation skills. Ability to perform data analytics including competence in mathematics and Microsoft Excel. Enjoys being part of a team and has strong team ethics. Analytical and problem-solving skills. A good understanding of Commerce.

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. additional_sections.content: 職務詳細（JobDetails） 職務目的（Job Purpose） 日本会計基準および米国会計基準の決算業務統括、経済価値ベースの保険負債評価 保険負債業務の推進と専門的サポート 内部統制の強化と業務改善 チームマネジメントと人材育成

四半期・年次決算業務の統括： 日本会計基準と米国会計基準に基づく決算業務全般を管理し、監査法人との対応に従事する。また、当局報告や財務開示資料作成における経理面からのサポートを行う。経済価値ベースの保険負債評価：ESR計測業務に従事する。保険会計業務の推進：保険負債評価においてアクチュアリー知見を活かした経理業務を実施する。内部統制の強化：決算業務の効率化と正確性向上のためのプロセス改善と法令遵守を行う。他部門との連携：損害率トレンドに関して定期的な意見交換を実施し、料率改定の有無を確認する。関連部門・シニアマネジメントへの報告：保険負債に関する主要事項の報告資料作成と分析結果の定期報告を行う。チームマネジメント：部メンバーの日常業務の指示、進捗管理、品質管理、研修を実施する。
内部関係先： Japan Chief Actuary、global/regional reserving actuary、保険数理部長、経理・財務部門、損害サービス・オペレーション・システム・再保険・リスク管理部。外部関係先： 日本アクチュアリー会、金融庁、業界団体。

title: Senior Claims Adjuster Marine
experience: Extensive experience in maritime insurance claims
required_skill_set: Experience managing lawyers, experts, and legal expenses
Ability to present to clients and internal stakeholders
Strong knowledge of maritime legal, regulatory, and procedural requirements
Experience with market IT systems and advanced Microsoft skills

additional_sections.content:
Senior Schadebehandelaar Marine – Rotterdam
Als een Marine Schadebehandelaar behandel je schadegevallen variërend van minder complexe zaken tot grote gebeurtenissen. De afhandeling begint vanaf de eerste melding en omvat het beoordelen van polis- en wettelijke aansprakelijkheid, je kwantumanalyse en leidt tot een tijdige en nauwkeurige afwikkeling, terwijl je de kostenrisico's beperkt. Je communiceert ontwikkelingen en uitkomsten indien nodig aan alle belangrijke belanghebbenden (intern en extern) en biedt een hoog serviceniveau aan klanten, terwijl je de Best Practices voor schadeafhandeling en wettelijke, regelgeving en nalevingsvereisten volgt.

Jouw toekomstige team
Stel je voor dat je een succesvolle carrière opbouwt bij AIG, samenwerkend met andere getalenteerde en zeer gemotiveerde collega’s die zich dagelijks inzetten om ons doel te bereiken: de meest toonaangevende verzekeringsmaatschappij ter wereld te worden. Wij geloven dat ons succes afhangt van het hebben van een team van wereldklasse.

Jouw verantwoordelijkheden:
- Breed intern en extern communiceren met belanghebbenden waar nodig.
- Bijdragen aan het onderhoud van Best Practice procedures voor maritieme claims, in lijn met wereldwijde Best Practices.
- Afhandelen van maritieme claims die binnen het afgesproken autorisatieniveau vallen.
- Terugkoppeling geven van trends in claims analyses aan het team en interne belanghebbenden.
- Sterke relaties opbouwen en onderhouden met underwriters en andere relevante zakelijke belanghebbenden in de regio.
- Bijwonen van claimsbesprekingen en reizen indien nodig.
- Ondersteunen van de Marine Claims Manager bij taken die gericht zijn op claimsprestaties en rapportages indien nodig.
- Ondersteunen van de Marine Claims Manager en het bredere team bij ad-hoc projecten indien nodig.
- Helpen bij het beheren van de dagelijkse operationele relatie/kwaliteitsborgingskwesties met Shared Services.
- Technische begeleiding en coaching bieden aan junior, minder ervaren collega’s van het wereldwijde maritieme claimsteam.
- ‘Thought leadership’ tonen door middel van training en presentaties aan zowel interne als externe doelgroepen.

Een persoon met uitgebreide ervaring in maritieme verzekeringsclaims.
Kennis van de door Marine gebruikte verzekeringstaal voor nauwkeurige en consistente interpretatie.
Ervaring in het beheren van advocaten, experts en juridische uitgaven.
Vermogen om te presenteren aan cliënten en interne belanghebbenden.
Sterke kennis van juridische, regelgevende en procesmatige vereisten specifiek voor maritieme zaken.
Vermogen om effectief bij te dragen binnen een teamomgeving.
Goede communicatieve vaardigheden.
Goede kennis en ervaring met markt-IT-systemen en geavanceerde vaardigheden in Microsoft-toepassingen.
Iemand die kansen ziet om processen en efficiëntie te verbeteren en in staat is om transformatieprojecten uit te voeren.
Een proactief en zelfstandig persoon.

AIG biedt een concurrerend primair en secundair arbeidsvoorwaardenpakket op basis van ervaring en functieniveau, vergezeld van andere voordelen zoals wereldwijde carrièremogelijkheden en uitgebreide trainings- en persoonlijke ontwikkelingsprogramma's.

American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is een toonaangevende wereldwijde verzekeringsorganisatie. Voortbouwend op 100 jaar ervaring, bieden de branche en partner ondernemingen van AIG vandaag de dag een breed scala aan schadeverzekeringen, levensverzekeringen, pensioenoplossingen en andere financiële diensten aan klanten in meer dan 80 landen en rechtsgebieden. Deze diverse aanbiedingen omvatten producten en diensten die bedrijven en individuen helpen om hun activa te beschermen, risico's te beheersen en voor pensioenzekerheid te zorgen. De Nederlandse organisatie heeft 120 medewerkers waarvan er 35 werkzaam zijn op de schadeafdeling. Je vormt een team van 2 behandelaren die Property schades zullen behandelen. Samen met de collega's van Marine zitten jullie in een team van 6 collega’s, de manager en een werkstudent.

Het is het beleid van AIG, haar dochterondernemingen en zusterbedrijven nu en voor de toekomst, om een gelijke kansen werkgever te zijn. Wij bieden gelijke kansen aan alle gekwalificeerde individuen, ongeacht ras, kleur, religie, leeftijd, geslacht, genderexpressie, nationale herkomst, handicap of enige andere wettelijk beschermde groep mensen. Bij AIG geloven wij dat diversiteit en inclusie van cruciaal belang zijn voor onze toekomst en onze missie - het creëren van een basis voor een creatieve werkplek die leidt tot innovatie, groei en winstgevendheid. Door een breed scala aan programma's en initiatieven investeren wij in elke werknemer, met als doel ervoor te zorgen dat onze mensen niet alleen als individuen worden gerespecteerd, maar ook werkelijk gewaardeerd worden om hun unieke perspectieven.

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike.

Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters
At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family.

Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world
American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become.

Welcome to a culture of inclusion
We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations.

additional_sections.content: Participate and collaborate in day-to-day business to develop the portfolio of Life Individual DM and Life Individual Non-DM with the underwriting process by ensuring it complies with the goals and objectives of production and profitability. Professionals with abilities towards proactivity, engagement and commitment are preferred. Underwriting: awareness of risk analysis and pricing which includes rate building, pricing methodology, claims process and requirements. Communication: strong ability to open communication and develop peer relations. Problem-solving: defining the problem, generating alternatives, evaluating, and selecting alternatives and implementing solutions. Negotiating: distribution abilities and negotiation skills to drive results towards sales targets. Operational support: optimizing work processes, identifying areas of opportunity to reduce variances in organization's processes, delivering high quality products and services meeting needs and requirements from internal and external customers based on delivering solid & consistent results.

Reports to the Life Manager, responsible for doing the underwriting process for DDM campaigns, support our DDM channels with all A&H commercial strategies, with priority, but not limited, on DDM business. Responsible for establishing good sponsors with brokers and caring about attracting new accounts and looking for the increase in sales in Life business. Analyze the potential businesses identified by the Distribution and/or Mass Dept. requested by the Brokers / Sponsor. Prepare the final quote and attractive renewal conditions for the Sponsor / Client and convenient for the company in terms of profitability and risk exposure according to their level of authority. Preparation of the Referral and authorization request in case the quote values exceed the local authority. Preparation of the Non-Standard Compensation document for PCC approval. Present the final quote to the commercial team, or together with the commercial executive, the Broker or Sponsor, when required. Negotiate policy conditions (new or renewals) reaching agreements as required by the Broker / Sponsor, always within the margins allowed by the company. Provide technical advice to the Broker/ Sponsor or Client when required. Generate and send the issuing request to the Operations Department for the issuance of policies. Verify the correct allocation of the products within the guidelines of the company. Carry out negotiations with assistance providers, within the benefits that are sought to be incorporated into our products. Generate, analyze, and present monthly subscription reports, key business indicators, and the massive portfolio.

Training: 3rd level degree in careers such as Business Administration, Finance, Economics, or related careers. Experience: 3 years of experience in similar positions in areas such as underwriting. Specialized Knowledge: Technical knowledge of insurance and underwriting of health care, life, and personal accident insurance products. Ingles level knowledge: medium (70%). Utilities: Advanced Excel. Proper communication, listening skills and interpretation. Ease of dealing with rejection. Efficient time management. Persistent. Ability to work as a team. Strong problem-solving skills. Willing and able to adapt to change. Ability to perform multiple tasks and work under pressure.

title: (AIG Sonpo) IT Support Manager
experience: 3+ years of relevant technology experience, including leadership; 5+ years team lead or project management experience; experience with ITSM, observability tools, large language models, and production system troubleshooting
educational_qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in related field
required_skill_set: Deep understanding of AI/machine learning concepts (algorithms, model training, deployment)
Proficiency in scripting languages (Python, Bash) and IaC tools (Terraform, Ansible)
Rapid incident diagnosis and resolution, including PIR
Data-driven system analysis and reliability improvement
Design, implementation, and operation of AI tools (chatbots, autonomous support agents)
Excellent communication skills in Japanese and English
Proactive learning of new technologies and methods
remote_type: On-site (primarily in the office, with emphasis on in-person collaboration)

additional_sections.content: The IT Support Manager is a critical role in our application support team, responsible for the operational health and performance of our applications. This role ensures high application availability and user satisfaction by swiftly resolving issues, performing root-cause analysis, and collaborating with our development team on fixes and improvements. You will bridge the gap between development and operations teams, fostering collaboration and shared ownership of reliability. Key responsibilities include defining and meeting Service Level Objectives (SLOs), managing error budgets, and conducting blameless postmortems for continuous improvement. Ultimately, strive to achieve a balance between the speed of software development and system stability, ensuring a seamless user experience.

Keep up continuous uptime and accessibility of critical business applications and services. This involves actively monitoring system performance, detecting potential issues, and implementing strategies to prevent downtime. Respond to and resolve incidents and outages promptly. This includes troubleshooting problems, coordinating with other teams, and restoring service quickly. Utilize AI and machine learning to analyze application logs and user behavior patterns, predicting potential issues and implementing proactive measures to prevent disruptions and performance degradation. Champion the adoption of AI technologies across the support organization. Provide training, create documentation, and establish best practices to upskill team members and foster a culture of AI innovation. Automate repetitive, manual tasks (toil) to improve efficiency and reduce human error. This might involve scripting, developing tools, and improving infrastructure management processes. Establish and maintain robust monitoring and alerting systems to gain real-time insights into system health and performance. This allows for proactive identification and detection of anomalies or potential issues. After major incidents causing outages, conduct blameless post-mortem reviews to analyze the root causes, document learnings, and implement corrective measures to prevent future occurrences. Work with cross-functional teams, including product, engineering, and business stakeholders, to identify high-impact opportunities for AI integration. Clearly articulate how AI solutions will improve efficiency, enhance the customer experience, and deliver measurable business outcomes. Establish clear, measurable targets for system performance and reliability, often based on Service Level Indicators (SLIs). These Service Level indicators and objectives guide development and operations priorities to maintain high levels of user satisfaction. Stay informed on emerging AI technologies and industry trends. Evaluate and pilot new AI solutions to continuously enhance application support processes and capabilities. Lead BAU projects and initiatives representing Japan application support team.

experience: Some underwriting experience, or relevant professional experience
required_skill_set: Customer and Sales oriented behaviors: motivated, tenacious, focused, technically skilled, proactive, and accountable

additional_sections.content: This role can be located in either our Chicago, IL office or our Cleveland, OH office. Underwriting Specialist, Retail Property. This role will be an ideal next step in your career if you already have some relevant experience in insurance – or this could be your entry into underwriting if you have transferable skills. Make your mark in Underwriting. We are reimagining how we help customers to manage risk, transforming our operating model, and reshaping our role responsibilities and career pathways. The goal is to unlock the full potential in each colleague – empowering our people to grow as insurance professionals and add more value to our customers and AIG. How you will create an impact: This role will focus on underwriting new and renewal business to help drive the profitable growth of our Retail Property line. You will be responsible for assessing the desirability of risk through regular contact with brokers, close co-ordination with AIG subject matter experts and robust reviews of claims history and general company information. Your relationships with brokers will be key to your success. You will need to provide excellent service to brokers through a consistent, timely, and proactive approach to underwriting. You will also aim to generate new business by expanding your broker network, attending broker events, and building new relationships across the market – in line with a sales plan established with your manager. Another key aspect of the role is the provision of accurate monthly management information including submissions, bind rate, average premium, and total premium. What you’ll need to succeed: Some underwriting experience, however, you are welcome to apply if you have other relevant professional experience. The ability to analyze financial statements. Strong communication, networking and relationship building skills. Good organizational and time management skills. Customer and Sales orientated behaviors: motivated, tenacious, focused, technically skilled, proactive, and accountable.

American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become.

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters: At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family.

We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories. We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com.

additional_sections.content: The Technology Risk & Controls Manager is an integral part of the Technology Risk and Controls (TRC) team responsible for risk management including audits, regulatory examinations, IT controls, governance, and system development. The role involves managing IT regulatory compliance, overseeing internal audit issues, enhancing key risk indicators, and implementing policies and standards.

Manage risk associated with transformation programs, monitor progress, and address risks. Ensure IT regulatory compliance through assessments, gap analysis, and action plans. Oversee internal audit issues, develop and monitor action plans. Extract and report key risk indicators monthly. Implement and update IT policies and standards, including training in Japan.

Minimum 10 years of experience in technology risk, control, governance, IT audit, or SOX in the financial industry. Bilingual in Japanese and English. Bachelor's degree required. Knowledge of SOX framework and internal/external controls in insurance. Ability to manage multiple stakeholders and processes. Strong analytical, communication, and negotiation skills.

AIG values in-person collaboration, offers comprehensive benefits through the Total Rewards Program, and promotes a culture of inclusion, diversity, and continuous learning. The company supports individual growth and community impact.

title: Property Claims Adjuster, TPA Oversight
experience: 4+ years of Property claims experience
required_skill_set: Experience with complex and high exposure property damage and Business Interruption (BI) claims
Technical expertise interpreting insurance contracts and policy language
In-depth knowledge of claim handling procedures, claims performance strategies, and claim best practices
Ability to initiate and champion change initiatives leveraging technology
Creativity in resolving challenging business problems

additional_sections.content: At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Property Claims Adjuster, TPA Oversight to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. Make your mark in TPA Claims Our claims teams are the proven problem solvers of choice for clients, delivering consistent technical excellence and showcasing our service differentiation to create an unparalleled global claims handling experience. Through a robust stakeholder feedback loop and supported by consistent processes and leadership, we take pride in delivering responsive, fair and professional service with empathy and efficiency. How you will create an impact Analyze and process first party property damage claims by investigating and gathering information to determine the exposure on the claim; ensure proactive claims handling aimed at the prompt and cost-effective resolution of claims through well-developed action plans. Determine need for and direct independent adjusters to gather information to determine exposure on the claim and control their costs. Identify and evaluate coverage issues; prepare comprehensive coverage letters with supervisory review and analysis; retain and provide direction to coverage counsel when necessary. Assess exposure and resolve claim within established evaluation. Maintain diaries and complete tasks within required timeframes as set forth by department guidelines; ensure claim files are timely and properly documented with clear and concise analysis on coverage, damages and reserves. Process and pay invoices within a timely manner for vendors. Coordinate vendor referrals for additional investigation and/or litigation management. Calculate and assign timely and appropriate reserves; monitor reserve accuracy throughout the life of the claim. Refer case as appropriate to supervisor and management. Respond to requests or directions in a professional and timely manner. Attend arbitrations and possibly mediations for settling claims. Successfully complete required background, state licensing examinations and continuing education requirements. Communicate with all internal and external business partners including underwriters, brokers and other claims management. Partner with TPA Governance and Relationship Management to help TPAs and insureds comply with claim handling and reporting guidelines. Work with TPA claims financial to check the accuracy of TPA financials ensuring alignment with TPA Oversight financials.

additional_sections.content: At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Global Quality Assurance Reviewer - Claims to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. Our Claims professionals support AIG policyholders through some of the most trying times. When our clients face losses large and small, we step up to help them ease the financial burdens of catastrophic events like fires and hurricanes; we help them get their businesses back up and running again or recoup the value of a lost family heirloom or a prized possession. Our Claims team is an industry leader, drawing daily on its unparalleled levels of expertise to offer our clients the highest levels of stewardship to drive total cost of claim improvement and consistent outcomes. AIG manages claims using a wealth of technical and market knowledge about risks, business practices, and the ever-evolving legal and regulatory environment.

Complete quality reviews on all files within program guidelines while achieving and maintaining calibrated application of Line of Business best practices and claim handling expectations. Organize and execute Benchmark reviews as a Team Lead; including scheduling, GQA review team oversite and results reporting. Establish and maintain effective communication with Line of Business partners including presentation of constructive feedback in a positive way. Identification of trends and improvement opportunities with follow-up to ensure implementation of meaningful continuous improvement and/or action plan in partnership with claims. Commitment to personal development and skill enhancement.

Additional language skills, desirable. Proven claims experience handling Personal Accident and Personal Lines LOB claims. Additional experience in other LOBs would be desirable. Excellent awareness of industry practices surrounding Claims Handling together with strong knowledge of subject matter and ability to apply it to the role. Experience in claims analysis and audit, desirable. Ability to make recommendations on action and improvement plans. Excellent attention to detail. Strong verbal and written Communication skills, ability to influence and to manage stakeholder expectations. Excellent organizational skills. Eligibility to work in US and Internationality - The role is based in the US and will require to work from other AIG offices (up to 25% travel).

educational_qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in science, Technology, Engineering or Mathematics, professional Engineer or Certified Fire Protection Specialist Designation is desired but not required

additional_sections.content: Risk Consulting at AIG is about teamwork! It is about utilizing your resources, working together to solve real world problems, and making a positive impact on our client’s futures. The role of Risk Engineering is critical to what we do at AIG, both as a risk management resource to our clients and as a leading global insurance organization. Risk Engineers are responsible for working with clients to identify and mitigate risks to their business such as fire, flood, windstorm, equipment breakdown etc.

Looking for variety and a challenge? One day you may be helping an Account Risk Engineer understand the interdependencies within an account, the next day you may be helping a Field Risk Engineering supervisor assign surveys or discussing your zone with the Head of Risk Consulting. The key to success isn’t about being the expert in every occupancy or on every topic, it’s about building trust and cross-functional relationships with stakeholders through effective communication.

Manage and lead a team of risk engineers within the DACH region who are responsible for: Completing account reviews and conducting surveys, Implement the Client Service Process, Managing risk improvements, Assist underwriting with technical assessment, Perform property loss prevention reviews of commercial property accounts, Manage training and personnel development within your team and establish and maintain a training and development plan, Work closely with underwriting to obtain and maintain clients and broker relationships, Ensure your team has the appropriate knowledge and skillsets to be effective in delivering services to underwriting and our clients, Implement the quality control of field surveys and account reviews completed by your team, Assist with Lead regional or global projects and audits, Provide engineering data system support/guidance and stay abreast of system related issues within zone, Coordinate engineering data system UAT (user acceptance testing) within region, Provide assistance to Regional Engineering Leader and Head of Risk Consulting.

Regular Diversity & Inclusion events as well as virtual sports activities, corporate benefits and bike leasing round off our working environment. Additional benefits such as 30 days of vacation per year, days off on December 24 and 31, company pension plans and capital-forming benefits are a matter of course for us.

title: テックマークジャパン株式会社　営業部東日本営業課　リーダー
experience: 3年以上の法人への提案（資料作成、商談、クロージング含む）実績
required_skill_set: ステークホルダーの折衝・調整スキル
既存顧客の深耕並びに新規開拓スキル

additional_sections.content: 【法人向け企画営業】：AIGグループおよび国内大手損害保険会社と連携を取り、延長保証制度を設計し、法人向けに展開します。各業界のトップクラスの企業における上層部の方々と接点があり、他業界では得られない成長実感がございます。◆入社後はAIGグループでの合同研修を行った後、それぞれの部署で現場研修を約2-3ヶ月間行います。現場研修が終わった後は配属部門にてOJTが行われます。◆新しい部署を経験できるジョブローテーションの可能性があり、将来的に様々なキャリアを築いていくことができます。◆家電メーカーや自動車メーカー、不動産会社、通販等のお客様の要望を細かくヒアリングしながら、どのような内容の保証にするのか営業部門とオペレーション部門が一緒になって企画から開発を行い、半年以上かけてひとつひとつのプログラムをカスタムメイドで作ります。経営者や役員と商談する機会もあり、高いビジネススキルを身につけることが可能となります。

無形商材である延長保証制度をお客様の新たな収益源や付加価値サービスとして、その価値を正しく提供し、当社・お客様の双方の収益を拡大していくことを目的とします。お客様に誠実に向き合いながら、延長保証制度のリスクを引き受ける損害保険会社とその代理店、当社委託先と信頼関係を築き、共に成長できる環境を作っていくプレイヤーとしての活動だけではなく、課・チームの目標達成に向けた管理サポート、メンバーのフォロー並びに育成に能動的に携わることが重要な役割です。
国内大手企業に対して延長保証制度の商品企画・立案・交渉・運営・契約に至るまでの提案型営業。既存顧客のアップセル並びにクロスセルによる深耕。新規顧客の開拓（ターゲットリスト作成含む）。延長保証制度に係る契約書・保証書・事務マニュアル等の作成、社内外の折衝。
売上げ目標の達成。ステークホルダー（顧客・保険会社・保険代理店・社内関係者）との円滑なコミュニケーション。各種資料作成の自己完結。

additional_sections.section_title: 求めるスキル・知識・経験
additional_sections.content: 【スキル】・必須 Outlook・WORD・EXCEL・Power Point等の基本的なPCスキル。ステークホルダー（社内外）の折衝・調整スキル。既存顧客の深耕並びに新規開拓スキル。・あればなお可 既存顧客の深耕並びに新規開拓スキル。【能力】・必須 視野を広く持ち積極的で明るくコミュニケーション力に長けている。1つのことに対して粘り強く自発的に取り組め、自己完結できる。
・必須 3年以上の法人への提案（資料作成、商談、クロージング含む）実績。社外並びに社内を取り纏め、課題解決や目標達成した実績。・あればなお可 営業企画の経験。保険業界（特に損害保険）での業務経験。延長保証の提案経験。

additional_sections.content: Support and independently review risks, prepare tailored offers for national and international clients, participate in expanding Financial Lines business in Germany, work with high responsibility in a supportive team, communicate in German and English with brokers, clients, and international colleagues, contribute to cross-departmental and strategic projects, and enjoy development opportunities.

Degree in Law or Economics or comparable qualification, at least initial experience in Financial Lines insurance, experience with brokers and intermediaries is advantageous, strong negotiation skills, fluent in German and English.

Supportive, inclusive, and innovative work environment, comprehensive benefits including health, wellbeing, and professional development, commitment to diversity and inclusion, equal opportunity employer, and support for reasonable accommodations.

title: Claims Adjuster - Aerospace
company_name: AIG Europe S.A. (U.K. Branch) & American International Group UK Ltd
experience: Prior experience in Aerospace insurance claims, handling losses from all Aerospace sub-classes in a lead capacity
required_skill_set: Managing external vendors including lawyers and surveyors
Legal and regulatory knowledge specific to Aerospace

additional_sections.content: Get to know the business General Insurance is a leading provider of insurance products and services for commercial and personal insurance customers. It includes one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. General Insurance offers a broad range of products to customers through a diversified, multichannel distribution network. Our Claims team is an industry leader, drawing daily on its unparalleled levels of expertise to offer our clients the highest levels of stewardship to drive total cost of claim improvement and consistent outcomes. AIG manages claims using a wealth of technical and market knowledge about risks, business practices, and the ever-evolving legal and regulatory environment.

Key claims handling, coverage determination, quantum analysis and legal liability assessment where appropriate and within authority limits. Work with the Head of Aerospace UK and the team to ensure effective vendor and litigation management on all lead claims. Broad internal stakeholder communication where required. Strive for continuous improvement on claim file handling with feedback and support through the Quality Assurance review processes. Contribute to maintenance of best practice procedures for Aerospace complex claims, consistent with global best practices. Handle Aerospace claims allocated within agreed level of authority limit. Demonstrate a high standard of technical claims competence and efficiency. Feedback claims trend analysis to team and internal stakeholders. Attend claims reviews and travel, as required.

A person with prior experience in Aerospace insurance claims. Experience of handling losses arising from all the Aerospace sub-classes in a lead capacity. Sound knowledge of policy language enabling accurate and consistent policy wording interpretation. Experience in managing external vendors including lawyers, surveyors for the benefit of the client, AIG and the market. Ability to deliver timely and accurate information to key stakeholders – the client, the broker, our underwriters and brokers, as well as claims management. Good knowledge of legal and regulatory requirements specific to Aerospace. A strong team player with excellent communication skills who thrives in a dynamic claims handling environment. Experience of market IT systems and ability analyse and interpret data from many sources.

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters. At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world. American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become. We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories. AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com.

title: Head of Personal Lines Claims
experience: Extensive experience in claims management
educational_qualifications: Bachelor’s Degree or above in related field

additional_sections.content: Join us as Head of Personal Line Claims to take your Claims career to the next level with a global market leader. Make your mark in Personal Line Claims Our Claims teams are the proven problem solvers of choice for clients, delivering consistent technical excellence and showcasing our service differentiation to create an unparalleled global claims handling experience. Through a robust stakeholder feedback loop and supported by consistent processes and leadership, we take pride in delivering responsive, fair and professional service with empathy and efficiency. Reporting to the Thailand Country Claims Manager, this position will be responsible for delivering the Personal Line Claims strategy (Auto, Personal Property, Extended Warranty, Plus), accountable for team performance and budget, and developing talent management. You balance the needs of multiple stakeholders, making sound decisions using data, analysis, experience, business concerns, judgment, along with a risk mindset. Ultimately, you take ownership for key outcomes.

Manage and lead the Personal Line Claims teams to achieve the goals and objectives as set. Manage overall relationships, communications and operational links with Profit Centre, Regional Office, Distribution channels, and business partner. Involve in activity and/or projects that affect Personal Insurance claim’s structure and workflow, including system related matters, to ensure operational efficiency. Lead the design and implementation Personal Line Claims strategies to support business aligned with Global/International claims, as well as Country business plans and priorities. Senior point of contact for claims for internal stakeholders & functions regarding to Personal Insurance Claims. Ensure quality and quantity management of claims in accordance with best practices. Manage performance standards and benchmarks. Manage and control total cost of claims, ensure that team expenses aligning and within budget. Ensure that claim staff respond to stakeholders with good attitude, good manner, fast and settle fairly and promptly. Manage resources, staff competency, claims workflow & process and service performance levels for the overall betterment and high level of team efficiency & effectiveness. Oversee the preparation and delivery of all claim management reports ensuring promptness and accuracy. Ensure that the team are operating within appropriate regulatory framework boundaries and protection of all licenses and agreements. Adhere to and embed the Company’s risk policies, Statement of Operating Standards and any AIG policies, audits, controls and ERM procedures.

Bachelor’s Degree or above in related field. Excellent problem solving and analytical skills. Excellent communication skills both verbal and written. Excellent leadership skills and interpersonal skills. Extensive experience in claims management. Well-developed negotiation skills. Sufficient people management and influencing skills. Calm and assertive manner when dealing with complaints.

title: Risk Engineer
company_name: AIG Europe S.A Filial i Sverige
experience: Few years property risk engineering experience (field risk engineering or account engineering)
educational_qualifications: University degree in engineering
required_skill_set: Broad knowledge in Risk Property controls
Experience in performing field risk surveys of commercial and light hazard industrial occupancies
Ability to read construction drawings, diagrams, printed materials
Ability to perform inspections of buildings and other structures, both visually and with instruments

additional_sections.content: AIG strives to be a highly client focused organization, something that should be reflected in your social skills, personal structure and high quality in every output of work performed. We are looking for a team player who is technically skilled in the property field.

Perform property loss prevention reviews of commercial property accounts, document review results, conduct client service meetings, analyze and manage risk improvements, translate client needs into service plans, estimate engineering service costs, review completed account reviews, conduct property surveys, identify hazards, assess external exposures, determine loss estimates, conduct consultative visits.

Other Nordic language skills such as Finnish or Danish are preferred. Soft skills include being highly motivated, decision-making ability, teamwork, and punctuality. Hard skills include professional engineer or fire protection specialist designation, inspection abilities, and reading construction drawings.

AIG values in-person collaboration, offers comprehensive benefits, promotes inclusion, and invests in employee development. The company operates across Stockholm, Copenhagen, Oslo, and Helsinki, fostering a culture of belonging and continuous learning.
